    49	
    50	/** Schema version for migration tracking */
    51	export const SCHEMA_VERSION = 3;
    52	
    53	/** SQL schema for code graph tables */
    54	const SCHEMA_SQL = `
    55	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_files (
    56	    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    57	    file_path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    58	    language TEXT NOT NULL,
    59	    content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    60	    file_mtime_ms INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    61	    node_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    62	    edge_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    63	    parse_health TEXT DEFAULT 'clean',
    64	    indexed_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    65	    parse_duration_ms INTEGER DEFAULT 0
    66	  );
    67	
    68	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_nodes (
    69	    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    70	    symbol_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    71	    file_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES code_files(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    72	    file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    73	    fq_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    74	    kind TEXT NOT NULL,
    75	    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    76	    start_line INTEGER NOT NULL,
    77	    end_line INTEGER NOT NULL,
    78	    start_column INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    79	    end_column INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    80	    language TEXT NOT NULL,
    81	    signature TEXT,
    82	    docstring TEXT,
    83	    content_hash TEXT NOT NULL
    84	  );
    85	
    86	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_edges (
    87	    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    88	    source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    89	    target_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    90	    edge_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    91	    weight REAL DEFAULT 1.0,
    92	    metadata TEXT
    93	  );
    94	
    95	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
    96	    version INTEGER NOT NULL
    97	  );
    98	
    99	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_graph_metadata (
   100	    key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   101	    value TEXT NOT NULL,
   102	    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   103	  );
   104	
   105	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_file_id ON code_nodes(file_id);
   106	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_symbol_id ON code_nodes(symbol_id);
   107	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_kind ON code_nodes(kind);
   108	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_name ON code_nodes(name);
   109	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_file_line ON code_nodes(file_path, start_line);
   110	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_source ON code_edges(source_id, edge_type);
   111	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_target ON code_edges(target_id, edge_type);
   112	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_type ON code_edges(edge_type);
   113	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_files_path ON code_files(file_path);
   114	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_files_hash ON code_files(content_hash);
   115	`;
   116	
   117	function getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath: string): number | null {
   118	  try {
   119	    return Math.trunc(statSync(filePath).mtimeMs);
   120	  } catch {
   121	    return null;
   122	  }
   123	}
   124	
   125	function hasColumn(database: Database.Database, tableName: string, columnName: string): boolean {
   126	  const rows = database.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
   127	  return rows.some((row) => row.name === columnName);
   128	}
   129	
   130	function ensureSchemaMigrations(database: Database.Database): void {
   131	  if (!hasColumn(database, 'code_files', 'file_mtime_ms')) {
   132	    database.exec('ALTER TABLE code_files ADD COLUMN file_mtime_ms INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0');
   133	  }
   134	
   135	  database.exec(`
   136	    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_graph_metadata (
   137	      key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   138	      value TEXT NOT NULL,
   139	      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   140	    );
   141	    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_file_line ON code_nodes(file_path, start_line);
   142	  `);
   143	}
   144	
   145	/** Initialize (or get) the code graph database */
   146	export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
   147	  if (db) return db;
   148	
   149	  try {
   150	    dbPath = join(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
   151	    db = new Database(dbPath);
   152	    db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); // WAL enables concurrent readers without locks
   153	    db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
   154	    db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
   155	    ensureSchemaMigrations(db);
   156	
   157	    const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
   158	    if (!versionRow) {
   159	      db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
   160	    } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
   161	      db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
   162	    }
   163	
   164	    return db;
   165	  } catch (err) {
   166	    if (db) {
   167	      try { db.close(); } catch { /* best effort cleanup for failed init */ }
   168	    }
   169	    db = null;
   170	    dbPath = null;
   171	    throw err;
   172	  }
   173	}
   174	
   175	/** Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed) */
   176	export function getDb(): Database.Database {
   177	  if (!db) initDb(DATABASE_DIR);
   178	  return db!;
   179	}
   180	
   181	/** Close the database connection */
   182	export function closeDb(): void {
   183	  if (db) {
   184	    db.close();
   185	    db = null;
   186	  }
   187	}
   188	
   189	function getMetadata(key: string): string | null {
   190	  const d = getDb();
   191	  const row = d.prepare('SELECT value FROM code_graph_metadata WHERE key = ?').get(key) as { value: string } | undefined;
   192	  return row?.value ?? null;
   193	}
   194	
   195	function setMetadata(key: string, value: string): void {
   196	  const d = getDb();
   197	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
   198	  d.prepare(`
   199	    INSERT INTO code_graph_metadata (key, value, updated_at)
   200	    VALUES (?, ?, ?)
   201	    ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value, updated_at = excluded.updated_at
   202	  `).run(key, value, now);
   203	}
   204	
   205	export function getLastGitHead(): string | null {
   206	  return getMetadata('last_git_head');
   207	}
   208	
   209	export function setLastGitHead(head: string): void {
   210	  setMetadata('last_git_head', head);
   211	}
   212	
   213	export function getLastDetectorProvenance(): DetectorProvenance | null {
   214	  const value = getMetadata('last_detector_provenance');
   215	  if (value === 'ast' || value === 'structured' || value === 'regex' || value === 'heuristic') {
   216	    return value;
   217	  }
   218	  return null;
   219	}
   220	
   221	export function setLastDetectorProvenance(provenance: DetectorProvenance): void {
   222	  setMetadata('last_detector_provenance', provenance);
   223	}
   224	
   225	export function getLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(): DetectorProvenanceSummary | null {
   226	  const value = getMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary');
   227	  if (!value) {
   228	    return null;
   229	  }
   230	
   231	  try {
   232	    return JSON.parse(value) as DetectorProvenanceSummary;
   233	  } catch {
   234	    return null;
   235	  }
   236	}
   237	
   238	export function setLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(summary: DetectorProvenanceSummary): void {
   239	  setMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary', JSON.stringify(summary));
   240	}
   241	
   242	export function getLastGraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary(): GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary | null {
   243	  const value = getMetadata('last_graph_edge_enrichment_summary');
   244	  if (!value) {
   245	    return null;
   246	  }
   247	
   248	  try {
   249	    return JSON.parse(value) as GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary;
   250	  } catch {
   251	    return null;
   252	  }
   253	}
   254	
   255	export function setLastGraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary(
   256	  summary: GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary,
   257	): void {
   258	  setMetadata('last_graph_edge_enrichment_summary', JSON.stringify(summary));
   259	}
   260	
   261	/** Insert or update a file record, returning the file ID */
   262	export function upsertFile(
   263	  filePath: string,
   264	  language: string,
   265	  contentHash: string,
   266	  nodeCount: number,
   267	  edgeCount: number,
   268	  parseHealth: string,
   269	  parseDurationMs: number,
   270	): number {
   271	  const d = getDb();
   272	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
   273	  const fileMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath) ?? 0;
   274	
   275	  const existing = d.prepare('SELECT id FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as { id: number } | undefined;
   276	  if (existing) {
   277	    d.prepare(`
   278	      UPDATE code_files SET language = ?, content_hash = ?, node_count = ?, edge_count = ?,
   279	        file_mtime_ms = ?, parse_health = ?, indexed_at = ?, parse_duration_ms = ?
   280	      WHERE id = ?
   281	    `).run(language, contentHash, nodeCount, edgeCount, fileMtimeMs, parseHealth, now, parseDurationMs, existing.id);
   282	    return existing.id;
   283	  }
   284	
   285	  const result = d.prepare(`
   286	    INSERT INTO code_files (
   287	      file_path, language, content_hash, file_mtime_ms, node_count, edge_count, parse_health, indexed_at, parse_duration_ms
   288	    )
   289	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   290	  `).run(filePath, language, contentHash, fileMtimeMs, nodeCount, edgeCount, parseHealth, now, parseDurationMs);
   291	  return Number(result.lastInsertRowid);
   292	}
   293	
   294	/** Batch insert nodes for a file (deletes existing first) */
   295	export function replaceNodes(fileId: number, nodes: CodeNode[]): void {
   296	  const d = getDb();
   297	  const insert = d.prepare(`
   298	    INSERT INTO code_nodes (symbol_id, file_id, file_path, fq_name, kind, name,
   299	      start_line, end_line, start_column, end_column, language, signature, docstring, content_hash)
   300	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   301	  `);
   302	
   303	  const tx = d.transaction(() => {
   304	    const oldSymbolRows = d.prepare('SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').all(fileId) as { symbol_id: string }[];
   305	    const oldSymbolIds = oldSymbolRows.map(row => row.symbol_id);
   306	
   307	    d.prepare('DELETE FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').run(fileId);
   308	
   309	    if (oldSymbolIds.length > 0) {
   310	      const placeholders = oldSymbolIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
   311	      d.prepare(`
   312	        DELETE FROM code_edges
   313	        WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})
   314	      `).run(...oldSymbolIds, ...oldSymbolIds);
   315	    }
   316	
   317	    for (const n of nodes) {
   318	      insert.run(
   319	        n.symbolId, fileId, n.filePath, n.fqName, n.kind, n.name,
   320	        n.startLine, n.endLine, n.startColumn, n.endColumn,

     1	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Db State
     3	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	
     5	import fs from 'fs/promises';
     6	import { resolveDatabasePaths, INDEX_SCAN_COOLDOWN } from './config.js';
     7	import type { DatabaseExtended } from '@spec-kit/shared/types';
     8	import type { GraphSearchFn } from '../lib/search/search-types.js';
     9	
    10	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	// 1. TYPES 
    12	
    13	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    14	
    15	/** Minimal vector index interface for database operations */
    16	type VectorSearchFn = (
    17	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
    18	  options: Record<string, unknown>
    19	) => Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
    20	
    21	export interface VectorIndexLike {
    22	  initializeDb(): void;
    23	  getDb(): DatabaseLike | null;
    24	  closeDb?(): void;
    25	  vectorSearch?: VectorSearchFn;
    26	  onDatabaseConnectionChange?: (listener: (database: DatabaseLike) => void) => (() => void);
    27	}
    28	
    29	/** Canonical DB type shared across MCP runtime modules */
    30	export type DatabaseLike = DatabaseExtended;
    31	
    32	/** Checkpoints module interface */
    33	export interface CheckpointsLike {
    34	  init(database: DatabaseLike): void;
    35	}
    36	
    37	/** Access tracker module interface */
    38	export interface AccessTrackerLike {
    39	  init(database: DatabaseLike): void;
    40	}
    41	
    42	/** Hybrid search module interface */
    43	export interface HybridSearchLike {
    44	  init(database: DatabaseLike, vectorSearch: VectorSearchFn | undefined, graphSearch?: GraphSearchFn | null): void;
    45	}
    46	
    47	/** Session manager module interface */
    48	export interface SessionManagerLike {
    49	  init(database: DatabaseLike): { success: boolean; error?: string };
    50	}
    51	
    52	/** Incremental index module interface */
    53	export interface IncrementalIndexLike {
    54	  init(database: DatabaseLike): void;
    55	}
    56	
    57	/** Generic DB consumer interface for modules that cache DB handles internally. */
    58	export interface DatabaseConsumerLike {
    59	  init(database: DatabaseLike): unknown;
    60	}
    61	
    62	/** Dependencies for db-state initialization */
    63	export interface DbStateDeps {
    64	  vectorIndex?: VectorIndexLike;
    65	  checkpoints?: CheckpointsLike;
    66	  accessTracker?: AccessTrackerLike;
    67	  hybridSearch?: HybridSearchLike;
    68	  sessionManager?: SessionManagerLike;
    69	  incrementalIndex?: IncrementalIndexLike;
    70	  graphSearchFn?: GraphSearchFn | null;
    71	  dbConsumers?: DatabaseConsumerLike[];
    72	}
    73	
    74	type DatabaseRebindListener = (database: DatabaseLike) => void;
    75	
    76	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    77	// 2. STATE VARIABLES 
    78	
    79	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    80	
    81	let lastDbCheck: number = 0;
    82	let reinitializeMutex: Promise<void> | null = null;
    83	let lastReinitializeSucceeded: boolean = true;
    84	let embeddingModelReady: boolean = false;
    85	let constitutionalCache: unknown = null;
    86	let constitutionalCacheTime: number = 0;
    87	let configTableCreated: boolean = false;
    88	const CONFIG_KEY_LAST_INDEX_SCAN = 'last_index_scan';
    89	const CONFIG_KEY_SCAN_STARTED_AT = 'scan_started_at';
    90	const DEFAULT_SCAN_LEASE_EXPIRY_MS = INDEX_SCAN_COOLDOWN * 2;
    91	
    92	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93	// 3. MODULE REFERENCES 
    94	
    95	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96	
    97	let vectorIndex: VectorIndexLike | null = null;
    98	let checkpoints: CheckpointsLike | null = null;
    99	let accessTracker: AccessTrackerLike | null = null;
   100	let hybridSearch: HybridSearchLike | null = null;
   101	let sessionManagerRef: SessionManagerLike | null = null;
   102	let incrementalIndexRef: IncrementalIndexLike | null = null;
   103	let graphSearchFnRef: GraphSearchFn | null | undefined = undefined;
   104	const dbConsumersRef: DatabaseConsumerLike[] = [];
   105	let vectorIndexListenerCleanup: (() => void) | null = null;
   106	let subscribedVectorIndex: VectorIndexLike | null = null;
   107	let suppressVectorIndexListener = false;
   108	const databaseRebindListeners = new Set<DatabaseRebindListener>();
   109	
   110	export function registerDatabaseRebindListener(listener: DatabaseRebindListener): () => void {
   111	  databaseRebindListeners.add(listener);
   112	  return () => {
   113	    databaseRebindListeners.delete(listener);
   114	  };
   115	}
   116	
   117	function notifyDatabaseRebindListeners(database: DatabaseLike): void {
   118	  for (const listener of databaseRebindListeners) {
   119	    try {
   120	      listener(database);
   121	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   122	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   123	      console.error(`[db-state] Database rebind listener failed: ${message}`);
   124	    }
   125	  }
   126	}
   127	
   128	function registerVectorIndexListener(nextVectorIndex: VectorIndexLike): void {
   129	  if (subscribedVectorIndex === nextVectorIndex) {
   130	    return;
   131	  }
   132	
   133	  if (vectorIndexListenerCleanup) {
   134	    vectorIndexListenerCleanup();
   135	    vectorIndexListenerCleanup = null;
   136	  }
   137	
   138	  subscribedVectorIndex = nextVectorIndex;
   139	  if (typeof nextVectorIndex.onDatabaseConnectionChange !== 'function') {
   140	    return;
   141	  }
   142	
   143	  vectorIndexListenerCleanup = nextVectorIndex.onDatabaseConnectionChange((database: DatabaseLike) => {
   144	    if (suppressVectorIndexListener) {
   145	      return;
   146	    }
   147	
   148	    try {
   149	      const rebound = rebindDatabaseConsumers(database);
   150	      if (!rebound) {
   151	        console.error('[db-state] Database consumer listener rebind returned false');
   152	      }
   153	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   154	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   155	      console.error(`[db-state] Database consumer listener rebind failed: ${message}`);
   156	    }
   157	  });
   158	}
   159	
   160	function rebindDatabaseConsumers(database: DatabaseLike): boolean {
   161	  if (checkpoints) checkpoints.init(database);
   162	  if (accessTracker) accessTracker.init(database);
   163	  if (hybridSearch) {
   164	    if (!graphSearchFnRef && process.env.SPECKIT_GRAPH_UNIFIED !== 'false') {
   165	      console.warn('[db-state] hybridSearch reinit missing graphSearchFn; graph retrieval channel is disabled');
   166	    }
   167	    hybridSearch.init(database, vectorIndex?.vectorSearch, graphSearchFnRef ?? null);
   168	  }
   169	  if (sessionManagerRef) {
   170	    const sessionInitResult = sessionManagerRef.init(database);
   171	    if (!sessionInitResult.success) {
   172	      const errorSuffix = sessionInitResult.error ? `: ${sessionInitResult.error}` : '';
   173	      console.error(`[db-state] Session manager rebind failed${errorSuffix}`);
   174	      return false;
   175	    }
   176	  }
   177	  if (incrementalIndexRef) incrementalIndexRef.init(database);
   178	  for (const consumer of dbConsumersRef) {
   179	    consumer.init(database);
   180	  }
   181	  notifyDatabaseRebindListeners(database);
   182	  return true;
   183	}
   184	
   185	function getDbUpdatedFilePath(): string {
   186	  return resolveDatabasePaths().dbUpdatedFile;
   187	}
   188	
   189	/** Initialize db-state with module dependencies for database lifecycle management. */
   190	export function init(deps: DbStateDeps): void {
   191	  if (deps.vectorIndex) {
   192	    vectorIndex = deps.vectorIndex;
   193	    registerVectorIndexListener(deps.vectorIndex);
   194	    // The backing DB handle may differ across init calls; force config table re-check.
   195	    configTableCreated = false;
   196	  }
   197	  if (deps.checkpoints) checkpoints = deps.checkpoints;
   198	  if (deps.accessTracker) accessTracker = deps.accessTracker;
   199	  if (deps.hybridSearch) hybridSearch = deps.hybridSearch;
   200	  if (deps.sessionManager) sessionManagerRef = deps.sessionManager;
   201	  if (deps.incrementalIndex) incrementalIndexRef = deps.incrementalIndex;
   202	  if (deps.graphSearchFn !== undefined) graphSearchFnRef = deps.graphSearchFn;
   203	  if (Array.isArray(deps.dbConsumers) && deps.dbConsumers.length > 0) {
   204	    for (const consumer of deps.dbConsumers) {
   205	      if (consumer && !dbConsumersRef.includes(consumer)) {
   206	        dbConsumersRef.push(consumer);
   207	      }
   208	    }
   209	  }
   210	}
   211	
   212	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   213	// 4. DATABASE CHANGE NOTIFICATION 
   214	
   215	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   216	
   217	/** Check if the database was updated externally and reinitialize if needed. */
   218	export async function checkDatabaseUpdated(): Promise<boolean> {
   219	  try {
   220	    const updateTimeRaw = await fs.readFile(getDbUpdatedFilePath(), 'utf8');
   221	    const updateTime = Number.parseInt(updateTimeRaw, 10);
   222	    if (!Number.isFinite(updateTime)) {
   223	      return false;
   224	    }
   225	
   226	    if (updateTime > lastDbCheck) {
   227	      console.error('[db-state] Database updated externally, reinitializing connection...');
   228	      const rebindSucceeded = await reinitializeDatabase(updateTime);
   229	      if (!rebindSucceeded) {
   230	        console.error('[db-state] Reinitialization did not complete; preserving lastDbCheck for retry');
   231	      }
   232	      return rebindSucceeded;
   233	    }
   234	  } catch (e: unknown) {
   235	    const code = e && typeof e === 'object' && 'code' in e ? (e as { code?: unknown }).code : undefined;
   236	    if (code === 'ENOENT' || code === 'EACCES') {
   237	      // Ignore missing marker or read errors.
   238	      return false;
   239	    }
   240	    throw e;
   241	  }
   242	  return false;
   243	}
   244	
   245	/** Close and reinitialize the database connection, refreshing all dependent module handles. */
   246	export async function reinitializeDatabase(updatedMarkerTime?: number): Promise<boolean> {
   247	  if (!vectorIndex) {
   248	    throw new Error('db-state not initialized: vector_index is null');
   249	  }
   250	
   251	  // If reinitialization is already in progress, wait for it
   252	  if (reinitializeMutex) {
   253	    console.error('[db-state] Reinitialization already in progress, waiting...');
   254	    await reinitializeMutex;
   255	    return lastReinitializeSucceeded;
   256	  }
   257	
   258	  let resolveMutex: () => void;
   259	  let rebindSucceeded = false;
   260	  reinitializeMutex = new Promise<void>(resolve => {

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Graph Search Fn
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Unified graph retrieval, deterministic ranking, explainability, and rollback
     5	// Causal graph search channel — uses FTS5 for node matching
     6	
     7	import { sanitizeFTS5Query } from './bm25-index.js';
     8	import { queryHierarchyMemories } from './spec-folder-hierarchy.js';
     9	import { registerDatabaseRebindListener } from '../../core/db-state.js';
    10	
    11	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    12	import type { GraphSearchFn } from './search-types.js';
    13	
    14	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	// 1. TYPES
    16	
    17	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    18	interface CausalEdgeRow {
    19	  id: string;
    20	  source_id: string;
    21	  target_id: string;
    22	  relation: string;
    23	  strength: number;
    24	}
    25	
    26	interface DegreeCacheState {
    27	  perNodeBoost: Map<string, number>;
    28	  maxTypedDegree: number | null;
    29	}
    30	
    31	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32	// 2. TYPED-DEGREE CONSTANTS
    33	
    34	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    35	/** Edge type weights for typed-degree computation (R4 5th RRF channel) */
    36	const EDGE_TYPE_WEIGHTS: Record<string, number> = {
    37	  caused: 1.0,
    38	  derived_from: 0.9,
    39	  enabled: 0.8,
    40	  contradicts: 0.7,
    41	  supersedes: 0.6,
    42	  supports: 0.5,
    43	};
    44	
    45	/** Fallback maximum typed degree when no edges exist in the database */
    46	const DEFAULT_MAX_TYPED_DEGREE = 15;
    47	
    48	/** Hard cap on raw typed degree before normalization */
    49	const MAX_TOTAL_DEGREE = 50;
    50	
    51	/** Maximum normalized boost score */
    52	const DEGREE_BOOST_CAP = 0.15;
    53	
    54	/** Runtime fusion weight for the degree channel. Keep aligned with the boost cap. */
    55	const DEGREE_CHANNEL_WEIGHT = DEGREE_BOOST_CAP;
    56	
    57	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    58	// 3. CAUSAL EDGE CHANNEL (FTS5-BACKED)
    59	
    60	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	let ftsTableAvailabilityPerDb = new WeakMap<Database.Database, boolean>();
    62	
    63	/**
    64	 * Check whether the FTS5 table exists in the database.
    65	 * Used to determine if FTS5 matching is available.
    66	 *
    67	 * Cache is scoped per bound DB instance to avoid repeated sqlite_master probes.
    68	 */
    69	function isFtsTableAvailable(database: Database.Database): boolean {
    70	  const cached = ftsTableAvailabilityPerDb.get(database);
    71	  if (typeof cached === 'boolean') {
    72	    return cached;
    73	  }
    74	
    75	  try {
    76	    const result = (database.prepare(
    77	      `SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'`
    78	    ) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
    79	    const available = !!result;
    80	    ftsTableAvailabilityPerDb.set(database, available);
    81	    return available;
    82	  } catch (_err: unknown) {
    83	    ftsTableAvailabilityPerDb.set(database, false);
    84	    return false;
    85	  }
    86	}
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * Find causal edges connected to memories matching the query.
    90	 *
    91	 * Uses FTS5 full-text search (memory_fts table) instead of naive LIKE matching.
    92	 * Falls back to LIKE only when the FTS5 table is not available.
    93	 */
    94	function queryCausalEdges(
    95	  database: Database.Database,
    96	  query: string,
    97	  limit: number,
    98	  specFolder?: string,
    99	): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
   100	  const graphResults: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];
   101	
   102	  try {
   103	    // Prefer FTS5 matching for proper full-text search
   104	    if (isFtsTableAvailable(database)) {
   105	      graphResults.push(...queryCausalEdgesFTS5(database, query, limit));
   106	    } else {
   107	      // Fallback: LIKE matching when FTS5 table is unavailable
   108	      graphResults.push(...queryCausalEdgesLikeFallback(database, query, limit));
   109	    }
   110	
   111	    // Hierarchy-aware fallback/augmentation for spec-scoped retrieval.
   112	    if (typeof specFolder === 'string' && specFolder.trim().length > 0) {
   113	      const hierarchyRows = queryHierarchyMemories(database, specFolder, Math.max(5, Math.ceil(limit / 2)));
   114	      for (const row of hierarchyRows) {
   115	        graphResults.push({
   116	          id: row.id,
   117	          score: Math.max(0, Math.min(1, row.relevance * 0.75)),
   118	          source: 'graph' as const,
   119	          sourceId: row.spec_folder,
   120	          targetId: specFolder,
   121	          relation: 'hierarchy',
   122	          title: row.title ?? `Hierarchy memory ${row.id}`,
   123	          specFolder: row.spec_folder,
   124	        });
   125	      }
   126	    }
   127	
   128	    // Deduplicate by memory id while preserving the highest score.
   129	    const deduped = new Map<number | string, Record<string, unknown>>();
   130	    for (const candidate of graphResults) {
   131	      const existing = deduped.get(candidate.id as number | string);
   132	      const existingScore = typeof existing?.score === 'number' ? existing.score : 0;
   133	      const nextScore = typeof candidate.score === 'number' ? candidate.score : 0;
   134	      if (!existing || nextScore > existingScore) {
   135	        deduped.set(candidate.id as number | string, candidate);
   136	      }
   137	    }
   138	
   139	    return Array.from(deduped.values())
   140	      .sort((a, b) => ((typeof b.score === 'number' ? b.score : 0) - (typeof a.score === 'number' ? a.score : 0)))
   141	      .slice(0, limit);
   142	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   143	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   144	    console.warn(`[graph-search-fn] Causal edge query failed: ${msg}`);
   145	    return [];
   146	  }
   147	}
   148	
   149	/**
   150	 * FTS5-backed causal edge query. Finds memory IDs via the memory_fts
   151	 * virtual table, then retrieves causal edges connected to those memories.
   152	 * Scores incorporate both edge strength and FTS5 BM25 relevance.
   153	 */
   154	function queryCausalEdgesFTS5(
   155	  database: Database.Database,
   156	  query: string,
   157	  limit: number
   158	): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
   159	  const sanitized = sanitizeFTS5Query(query);
   160	  if (!sanitized) return [];
   161	  const oversampleLimit = limit * 3;
   162	
   163	  // BM25-inspired weights: title(10) highest signal, content(5), triggers(2), folder(1).
   164	  // Query shape:
   165	  // 1) Materialize matched memory rowids once (no OR join against memory_fts)
   166	  // 2) UNION ALL source-side and target-side edge lookups
   167	  // 3) Collapse duplicate edge hits in SQL (MAX fts_score per edge)
   168	  const rows = (database.prepare(`
   169	    WITH matched_memories AS (
   170	      SELECT
   171	        rowid AS memory_id,
   172	        -bm25(memory_fts, 10.0, 5.0, 2.0, 1.0) AS fts_score
   173	      FROM memory_fts
   174	      WHERE memory_fts MATCH ?
   175	      ORDER BY fts_score DESC
   176	      LIMIT ?
   177	    ),
   178	    edge_hits AS (
   179	      SELECT ce.id, ce.source_id, ce.target_id, ce.relation, ce.strength, mm.fts_score
   180	      FROM matched_memories mm
   181	      JOIN causal_edges ce ON ce.source_id = CAST(mm.memory_id AS TEXT)
   182	      UNION ALL
   183	      SELECT ce.id, ce.source_id, ce.target_id, ce.relation, ce.strength, mm.fts_score
   184	      FROM matched_memories mm
   185	      JOIN causal_edges ce ON ce.target_id = CAST(mm.memory_id AS TEXT)
   186	    ),
   187	    scored_edges AS (
   188	      SELECT
   189	        id,
   190	        source_id,
   191	        target_id,
   192	        relation,
   193	        strength,
   194	        MAX(fts_score) AS fts_score
   195	      FROM edge_hits
   196	      GROUP BY id, source_id, target_id, relation, strength
   197	    )
   198	    SELECT id, source_id, target_id, relation, strength, fts_score
   199	    FROM scored_edges
   200	    ORDER BY (strength * fts_score) DESC
   201	    LIMIT ?
   202	  `) as Database.Statement).all(
   203	    sanitized,
   204	    oversampleLimit,
   205	    oversampleLimit,
   206	  ) as Array<CausalEdgeRow & { fts_score: number }>;
   207	
   208	  // Return one candidate entry per memory node (source_id and target_id) with
   209	  // Numeric IDs matching memory_index.id (INTEGER column) in the hybrid search
   210	  // Pipeline (MMR reranking filters with typeof id === 'number').
   211	  const candidates: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];
   212	  for (const row of rows) {
   213	    const edgeStrength = typeof row.strength === 'number'
   214	      ? Math.min(1, Math.max(0, row.strength))
   215	      : 0;
   216	    const ftsScore = typeof row.fts_score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.fts_score)
   217	      ? row.fts_score
   218	      : 0;
   219	    const score = edgeStrength * ftsScore;
   220	    const title = `${row.source_id} -> ${row.target_id}`;
   221	
   222	    const sourceNum = Number(row.source_id);
   223	    if (!Number.isNaN(sourceNum)) {
   224	      candidates.push({
   225	        id: sourceNum,
   226	        score,
   227	        edgeStrength,
   228	        ftsScore,
   229	        source: 'graph' as const,
   230	        title,
   231	        relation: row.relation,
   232	        sourceId: row.source_id,
   233	        targetId: row.target_id,
   234	      });
   235	    }
   236	
   237	    const targetNum = Number(row.target_id);
   238	    if (!Number.isNaN(targetNum) && targetNum !== sourceNum) {
   239	      candidates.push({
   240	        id: targetNum,
   241	        score,
   242	        edgeStrength,
   243	        ftsScore,
   244	        source: 'graph' as const,
   245	        title,
   246	        relation: row.relation,
   247	        sourceId: row.source_id,
   248	        targetId: row.target_id,
   249	      });
   250	    }
   251	  }
   252	  return candidates.slice(0, limit);
   253	}
   254	
   255	/**
   256	 * Legacy LIKE-based fallback when FTS5 table is unavailable.
   257	 */
   258	function queryCausalEdgesLikeFallback(
   259	  database: Database.Database,
   260	  query: string,
   261	  limit: number
   262	): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
   263	  const escaped = query.replace(/[%_]/g, '\\$&');
   264	  const likeParam = `%${escaped}%`;
   265	  const oversampleLimit = limit * 3;
   266	
   267	  const rows = (database.prepare(`
   268	    SELECT ce.id, ce.source_id, ce.target_id, ce.relation, ce.strength
   269	    FROM causal_edges ce
   270	    WHERE ce.source_id IN (
   271	      SELECT id
   272	      FROM memory_index
   273	      WHERE (content_text LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\' OR title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')
   274	    )
   275	       OR ce.target_id IN (
   276	      SELECT id
   277	      FROM memory_index
   278	      WHERE (content_text LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\' OR title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')
   279	    )
   280	    ORDER BY ce.strength DESC
   281	    LIMIT ?
   282	  `) as Database.Statement).all(
   283	    likeParam,
   284	    likeParam,
   285	    likeParam,
   286	    likeParam,
   287	    oversampleLimit,
   288	  ) as CausalEdgeRow[];
   289	
   290	  // Return one candidate entry per memory node (source_id and target_id) with
   291	  // Numeric IDs matching memory_index.id (INTEGER column).
   292	  const candidates: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];
   293	  for (const row of rows) {
   294	    const score = typeof row.strength === 'number' ? Math.min(1, Math.max(0, row.strength)) : 0;
   295	    const title = `${row.source_id} -> ${row.target_id}`;
   296	
   297	    const sourceNum = Number(row.source_id);
   298	    if (!Number.isNaN(sourceNum)) {
   299	      candidates.push({
   300	        id: sourceNum,
   301	        score,
   302	        source: 'graph' as const,
   303	        title,
   304	        relation: row.relation,
   305	        sourceId: row.source_id,
   306	        targetId: row.target_id,
   307	      });
   308	    }
   309	
   310	    const targetNum = Number(row.target_id);
   311	    if (!Number.isNaN(targetNum) && targetNum !== sourceNum) {
   312	      candidates.push({
   313	        id: targetNum,
   314	        score,
   315	        source: 'graph' as const,
   316	        title,
   317	        relation: row.relation,
   318	        sourceId: row.source_id,
   319	        targetId: row.target_id,
   320	      });
   321	    }
   322	  }
   323	  return candidates;
   324	}
   325	
   326	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   327	// 4. TYPED-DEGREE COMPUTATION
   328	
   329	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   330	/**
   331	 * In-memory degree cache scoped per bound DB instance.
   332	 * Stores both per-node boosted scores and cached global max typed degree.
   333	 * Invalidated via clearDegreeCache() on causal edge mutations.
   334	 *
   335	 * A4-P2-1: Edge materialization optimization — investigated and found adequate.
   336	 * Degree scores are cached per-session via this Map and invalidated on edge
   337	 * mutations (insert/update/delete) through clearDegreeCache() called from
   338	 * causal-edges.ts.  Community detection in community-detection.ts uses a
   339	 * separate debounce (lastEdgeCount + computedThisSession) to skip
   340	 * re-computation when the graph is unchanged.  No additional optimization

 succeeded in 0ms:
   560	        }
   561	      }
   562	
   563	      return changed;
   564	    })();
   565	
   566	    if (changed) {
   567	      invalidateDegreeCache();
   568	    }
   569	
   570	    return changed;
   571	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   572	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   573	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] updateEdge error: ${msg}`);
   574	    return false;
   575	  }
   576	}
   577	
   578	function deleteEdge(edgeId: number): boolean {
   579	  if (!db) return false;
   580	
   581	  try {
   582	    const result = (db.prepare(
   583	      'DELETE FROM causal_edges WHERE id = ?'
   584	    ) as Database.Statement).run(edgeId);
   585	    if ((result as { changes: number }).changes > 0) {
   586	      invalidateDegreeCache();
   587	    }
   588	    return (result as { changes: number }).changes > 0;
   589	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   590	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   591	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] deleteEdge error: ${msg}`);
   592	    return false;
   593	  }
   594	}
   595	
   596	// Let errors propagate so callers inside transactions see failures.
   597	// Previously errors were caught and swallowed, which hid edge-cleanup failures
   598	// From transactional callers (e.g., memory-bulk-delete, memory-crud-delete).
   599	function deleteEdgesForMemory(memoryId: string): number {
   600	  if (!db) return 0;
   601	
   602	  const result = (db.prepare(`
   603	    DELETE FROM causal_edges
   604	    WHERE source_id = ? OR target_id = ?
   605	  `) as Database.Statement).run(memoryId, memoryId);
   606	  if ((result as { changes: number }).changes > 0) {
   607	    invalidateDegreeCache();
   608	  }
   609	  return (result as { changes: number }).changes;
   610	}
   611	
   612	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   613	   8. STATS & QUERIES
   614	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   615	
   616	function getGraphStats(): GraphStats {
   617	  if (!db) {
   618	    return { totalEdges: 0, byRelation: {}, avgStrength: 0, uniqueSources: 0, uniqueTargets: 0 };
   619	  }
   620	
   621	  try {
   622	    const total = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
   623	    const byRelation = (db.prepare('SELECT relation, COUNT(*) as count FROM causal_edges GROUP BY relation') as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{ relation: string; count: number }>;
   624	    const avgStrength = (db.prepare('SELECT AVG(strength) as avg FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { avg: number | null };
   625	    const sources = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT source_id) as count FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
   626	    const targets = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT target_id) as count FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
   627	
   628	    const relationMap: Record<string, number> = {};
   629	    for (const row of byRelation) {
   630	      relationMap[row.relation] = row.count;
   631	    }
   632	
   633	    return {
   634	      totalEdges: total.count,
   635	      byRelation: relationMap,
   636	      avgStrength: Math.round((avgStrength.avg || 0) * 100) / 100,
   637	      uniqueSources: sources.count,
   638	      uniqueTargets: targets.count,
   639	    };
   640	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   641	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   642	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getGraphStats error: ${msg}`);
   643	    return { totalEdges: 0, byRelation: {}, avgStrength: 0, uniqueSources: 0, uniqueTargets: 0 };
   644	  }
   645	}
   646	
   647	function findOrphanedEdges(): CausalEdge[] {
   648	  if (!db) return [];
   649	
   650	  try {
   651	    return (db.prepare(`
   652	      SELECT ce.* FROM causal_edges ce
   653	      WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM memory_index m WHERE CAST(m.id AS TEXT) = ce.source_id)
   654	        OR NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM memory_index m WHERE CAST(m.id AS TEXT) = ce.target_id)
   655	    `) as Database.Statement).all() as CausalEdge[];
   656	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   657	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   658	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] findOrphanedEdges error: ${msg}`);
   659	    return [];
   660	  }
   661	}
   662	
   663	// Automated orphan edge cleanup
   664	function cleanupOrphanedEdges(): { deleted: number } {
   665	  if (!db) return { deleted: 0 };
   666	  try {
   667	    const orphaned = findOrphanedEdges();
   668	    const deleted = runInTransaction(db, () => {
   669	      let count = 0;
   670	      for (const edge of orphaned) {
   671	        if (deleteEdge(edge.id)) count++;
   672	      }
   673	      return count;
   674	    });
   675	    return { deleted };
   676	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   677	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   678	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] cleanupOrphanedEdges error: ${msg}`);
   679	    return { deleted: 0 };
   680	  }
   681	}
   682	
   683	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   684	   9. SPEC DOCUMENT CHAINS (Spec 126)
   685	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   686	
   687	/**
   688	 * Create causal relationship chain between spec folder documents.
   689	 * Links: spec -> plan (CAUSED), plan -> tasks (CAUSED), tasks -> impl-summary (CAUSED)
   690	 * Also: checklist SUPPORTS spec, decision-record SUPPORTS plan, research SUPPORTS spec
   691	 *
   692	 * @param documentIds Map of document_type -> memory_index.id for documents in the same spec folder
   693	 */
   694	function createSpecDocumentChain(documentIds: Record<string, number>): { inserted: number; failed: number } {
   695	  if (!db) return { inserted: 0, failed: 0 };
   696	
   697	  const edges: Array<{
   698	    sourceId: string;
   699	    targetId: string;
   700	    relation: RelationType;
   701	    strength?: number;
   702	    evidence?: string | null;
   703	  }> = [];
   704	
   705	  const ids = documentIds;
   706	
   707	  // Primary chain: spec -> plan -> tasks -> implementation_summary
   708	  if (ids.spec && ids.plan) {
   709	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.spec), targetId: String(ids.plan), relation: RELATION_TYPES.CAUSED, strength: 0.9, evidence: 'Spec 126: spec -> plan chain' });
   710	  }
   711	  if (ids.plan && ids.tasks) {
   712	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.plan), targetId: String(ids.tasks), relation: RELATION_TYPES.CAUSED, strength: 0.9, evidence: 'Spec 126: plan -> tasks chain' });
   713	  }
   714	  if (ids.tasks && ids.implementation_summary) {
   715	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.tasks), targetId: String(ids.implementation_summary), relation: RELATION_TYPES.CAUSED, strength: 0.8, evidence: 'Spec 126: tasks -> impl-summary chain' });
   716	  }
   717	
   718	  // Support relationships
   719	  if (ids.checklist && ids.spec) {
   720	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.checklist), targetId: String(ids.spec), relation: RELATION_TYPES.SUPPORTS, strength: 0.7, evidence: 'Spec 126: checklist supports spec' });
   721	  }
   722	  if (ids.decision_record && ids.plan) {
   723	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.decision_record), targetId: String(ids.plan), relation: RELATION_TYPES.SUPPORTS, strength: 0.8, evidence: 'Spec 126: decision-record supports plan' });
   724	  }
   725	  if (ids.research && ids.spec) {
   726	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.research), targetId: String(ids.spec), relation: RELATION_TYPES.SUPPORTS, strength: 0.7, evidence: 'Spec 126: research supports spec' });
   727	  }
   728	
   729	  if (edges.length === 0) return { inserted: 0, failed: 0 };
   730	
   731	  return insertEdgesBatch(edges);
   732	}
   733	
   734	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   735	   10. WEIGHT HISTORY & AUDIT (T001d)
   736	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   737	
   738	function logWeightChange(
   739	  edgeId: number,
   740	  oldStrength: number,
   741	  newStrength: number,
   742	  changedBy: string = 'manual',
   743	  reason: string | null = null,
   744	): void {
   745	  if (!db) return;
   746	  (db.prepare(`
   747	    INSERT INTO weight_history (edge_id, old_strength, new_strength, changed_by, reason)
   748	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   749	  `) as Database.Statement).run(edgeId, oldStrength, newStrength, changedBy, reason);
   750	}
   751	
   752	function getWeightHistory(edgeId: number, limit: number = 50): WeightHistoryEntry[] {
   753	  if (!db) return [];
   754	  try {
   755	    return (db.prepare(`
   756	      SELECT * FROM weight_history
   757	      WHERE edge_id = ?
   758	      ORDER BY changed_at DESC, id DESC
   759	      LIMIT ?
   760	    `) as Database.Statement).all(edgeId, limit) as WeightHistoryEntry[];
   761	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   762	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   763	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getWeightHistory error: ${msg}`);
   764	    return [];
   765	  }
   766	}
   767	
   768	function rollbackWeights(edgeId: number, toTimestamp: string): boolean {
   769	  if (!db) return false;
   770	  const database = db;
   771	  try {
   772	    // Wrap SELECTs + UPDATE + logWeightChange in a transaction for atomicity
   773	    const changed = database.transaction(() => {
   774	      // Get current strength before rollback
   775	      const current = (database.prepare(
   776	        'SELECT strength FROM causal_edges WHERE id = ?'
   777	      ) as Database.Statement).get(edgeId) as { strength: number } | undefined;
   778	      if (!current) return null;
   779	
   780	      // Prefer the latest exact-timestamp row to make same-second
   781	      // History entries deterministic when callers pass a value from getWeightHistory().
   782	      let entry = (database.prepare(`
   783	        SELECT old_strength FROM weight_history
   784	        WHERE edge_id = ? AND changed_at = ?
   785	        ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1
   786	      `) as Database.Statement).get(edgeId, toTimestamp) as { old_strength: number } | undefined;
   787	
   788	      // Find the earliest history entry strictly after the target timestamp.
   789	      // This preserves legacy "rollback to before next change" semantics for
   790	      // Timestamps that do not exactly match a stored history row.
   791	      if (!entry) {
   792	        entry = (database.prepare(`
   793	        SELECT old_strength FROM weight_history
   794	        WHERE edge_id = ? AND changed_at > ?
   795	        ORDER BY changed_at ASC, id ASC LIMIT 1
   796	      `) as Database.Statement).get(edgeId, toTimestamp) as { old_strength: number } | undefined;
   797	      }
   798	
   799	      if (!entry) {
   800	        // Fall back: get the oldest entry's old_strength (pre-change baseline)
   801	        entry = (database.prepare(`
   802	          SELECT old_strength FROM weight_history
   803	          WHERE edge_id = ?
   804	          ORDER BY changed_at ASC, id ASC LIMIT 1
   805	        `) as Database.Statement).get(edgeId) as { old_strength: number } | undefined;
   806	      }
   807	      if (!entry) return null;
   808	
   809	      // Restore the edge to the old_strength value
   810	      const result = (database.prepare(
   811	        'UPDATE causal_edges SET strength = ? WHERE id = ?'
   812	      ) as Database.Statement).run(entry.old_strength, edgeId);
   813	
   814	      // Log the rollback
   815	      if (isFiniteNumber(current.strength) && current.strength !== entry.old_strength) {
   816	        logWeightChange(edgeId, current.strength, entry.old_strength, 'rollback', `rollback to ${toTimestamp}`);
   817	      }
   818	
   819	      return (result as { changes: number }).changes > 0;
   820	    })();
   821	
   822	    if (changed === null) return false;
   823	    invalidateDegreeCache();
   824	    return changed;
   825	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   826	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   827	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] rollbackWeights error: ${msg}`);
   828	    return false;
   829	  }
   830	}
   831	
   832	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   833	   11. EDGE BOUNDS & COUNTING (N3-lite NFR-R01)
   834	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   835	
   836	function countEdgesForNode(nodeId: string): number {
   837	  if (!db) return 0;
   838	  try {
   839	    const row = (db.prepare(`
   840	      SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM causal_edges
   841	      WHERE source_id = ? OR target_id = ?
   842	    `) as Database.Statement).get(nodeId, nodeId) as { count: number };
   843	    return row.count;
   844	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   845	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   846	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] countEdgesForNode error: ${msg}`);
   847	    return 0;
   848	  }
   849	}
   850	
   851	function touchEdgeAccess(edgeId: number): void {
   852	  if (!db) return;
   853	  (db.prepare(
   854	    "UPDATE causal_edges SET last_accessed = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?"
   855	  ) as Database.Statement).run(edgeId);
   856	}
   857	
   858	function getStaleEdges(thresholdDays: number = STALENESS_THRESHOLD_DAYS): CausalEdge[] {
   859	  if (!db) return [];
   860	  try {
   861	    return (db.prepare(`
   862	      SELECT * FROM causal_edges
   863	      WHERE (last_accessed IS NULL AND extracted_at < datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days'))
   864	         OR (last_accessed IS NOT NULL AND last_accessed < datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days'))
   865	      ORDER BY COALESCE(last_accessed, extracted_at) ASC
   866	    `) as Database.Statement).all(thresholdDays, thresholdDays) as CausalEdge[];
   867	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   868	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   869	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getStaleEdges error: ${msg}`);
   870	    return [];
   871	  }
   872	}
   873	
   874	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   875	   12. EXPORTS
   876	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   877	
   878	export {
   879	  RELATION_TYPES,
   880	  RELATION_WEIGHTS,
   881	  DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH,
   882	  MAX_EDGES_LIMIT,
   883	  MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE,
   884	  MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH,
   885	  MAX_STRENGTH_INCREASE_PER_CYCLE,
   886	  STALENESS_THRESHOLD_DAYS,
   887	  DECAY_STRENGTH_AMOUNT,
   888	  DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS,
   889	
   890	  init,
   891	  insertEdge,
   892	  insertEdgesBatch,
   893	  bulkInsertEdges,
   894	  getEdgesFrom,
   895	  getEdgesTo,
   896	  getAllEdges,
   897	  getCausalChain,
   898	  updateEdge,
   899	  deleteEdge,
   900	  deleteEdgesForMemory,
   901	  getGraphStats,
   902	  findOrphanedEdges,
   903	  cleanupOrphanedEdges,
   904	  createSpecDocumentChain,
   905	
   906	  // T001d: Weight history & audit
   907	  logWeightChange,
   908	  getWeightHistory,
   909	  rollbackWeights,
   910	
   911	  // N3-lite: Edge bounds & counting
   912	  countEdgesForNode,
   913	  touchEdgeAccess,
   914	  getStaleEdges,
   915	};
   916	
   917	/**
   918	 * Re-exports related public types.
   919	 */
   920	export type {

 succeeded in 0ms:
   258	function insertEdgesBatch(
   259	  edges: Array<{
   260	    sourceId: string;
   261	    targetId: string;
   262	    relation: RelationType;
   263	    strength?: number;
   264	    evidence?: string | null;
   265	    createdBy?: string;
   266	  }>
   267	): { inserted: number; failed: number } {
   268	  if (!db) return { inserted: 0, failed: edges.length };
   269	
   270	  let inserted = 0;
   271	  let failed = 0;
   272	
   273	  const insertTx = db.transaction(() => {
   274	    for (const edge of edges) {
   275	      const id = insertEdge(
   276	        edge.sourceId,
   277	        edge.targetId,
   278	        edge.relation,
   279	        edge.strength ?? 1.0,
   280	        edge.evidence ?? null,
   281	        false,
   282	        edge.createdBy ?? 'manual',
   283	      );
   284	      if (id !== null) inserted++;
   285	      else failed++;
   286	    }
   287	  });
   288	
   289	  try {
   290	    insertTx();
   291	    if (inserted > 0) {
   292	      invalidateDegreeCache();
   293	    }
   294	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   295	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   296	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] insertEdgesBatch error: ${msg}`);
   297	  }
   298	
   299	  return { inserted, failed };
   300	}
   301	
   302	function bulkInsertEdges(edges: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): { inserted: number; failed: number } {
   303	  if (!db) return { inserted: 0, failed: edges.length };
   304	
   305	  const database = db;
   306	  let inserted = 0;
   307	  let failed = 0;
   308	
   309	  try {
   310	    const edgeColumns = (database.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(causal_edges)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>)
   311	      .map((column) => column.name)
   312	      .filter((name) => typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0 && name !== 'id');
   313	
   314	    if (edgeColumns.length === 0) {
   315	      return { inserted: 0, failed: edges.length };
   316	    }
   317	
   318	    const insertEdgeStmt = database.prepare(`
   319	      INSERT OR IGNORE INTO causal_edges (${edgeColumns.join(', ')})
   320	      VALUES (${edgeColumns.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
   321	    `) as Database.Statement;
   322	
   323	    const insertTx = database.transaction(() => {
   324	      for (const edge of edges) {
   325	        if (!edge || typeof edge.source_id !== 'string' || typeof edge.target_id !== 'string') {
   326	          failed++;
   327	          continue;
   328	        }
   329	        if (typeof edge.relation !== 'string' || edge.relation.trim().length === 0) {
   330	          failed++;
   331	          continue;
   332	        }
   333	        if (edge.source_id === edge.target_id) {
   334	          failed++;
   335	          continue;
   336	        }
   337	
   338	        const result = insertEdgeStmt.run(...edgeColumns.map((column) => edge[column] ?? null)) as { changes: number };
   339	        if (result.changes > 0) {
   340	          inserted++;
   341	        }
   342	      }
   343	    });
   344	
   345	    insertTx();
   346	    if (inserted > 0) {
   347	      invalidateDegreeCache();
   348	    }
   349	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   350	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   351	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] bulkInsertEdges error: ${msg}`);
   352	  }
   353	
   354	  return { inserted, failed };
   355	}
   356	
   357	function createEdgeQueryResult(
   358	  edges: CausalEdge[],
   359	  limit: number,
   360	  truncated: boolean,
   361	): EdgeQueryResult {
   362	  const result = edges as EdgeQueryResult;
   363	  result.truncated = truncated;
   364	  result.limit = limit;
   365	  return result;
   366	}
   367	
   368	function getEdgesFrom(sourceId: string, limit: number = MAX_EDGES_LIMIT): EdgeQueryResult {
   369	  if (!db) return createEdgeQueryResult([], limit, false);
   370	
   371	  try {
   372	    const rows = (db.prepare(`
   373	      SELECT * FROM causal_edges
   374	      WHERE source_id = ?
   375	      ORDER BY strength DESC
   376	      LIMIT ?
   377	    `) as Database.Statement).all(sourceId, limit + 1) as CausalEdge[];
   378	    const truncated = rows.length > limit;
   379	    const edges = truncated ? rows.slice(0, limit) : rows;
   380	    for (const edge of edges) {
   381	      try { touchEdgeAccess(edge.id); } catch (e: unknown) {
   382	        console.warn(`[causal-edges] touchEdgeAccess failed for edge ${edge.id}: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`);
   383	      }
   384	    }
   385	    return createEdgeQueryResult(edges, limit, truncated);
   386	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   387	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   388	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getEdgesFrom error: ${msg}`);
   389	    return createEdgeQueryResult([], limit, false);
   390	  }
   391	}
   392	
   393	function getEdgesTo(targetId: string, limit: number = MAX_EDGES_LIMIT): EdgeQueryResult {
   394	  if (!db) return createEdgeQueryResult([], limit, false);
   395	
   396	  try {
   397	    const rows = (db.prepare(`
   398	      SELECT * FROM causal_edges
   399	      WHERE target_id = ?
   400	      ORDER BY strength DESC
   401	      LIMIT ?
   402	    `) as Database.Statement).all(targetId, limit + 1) as CausalEdge[];
   403	    const truncated = rows.length > limit;
   404	    const edges = truncated ? rows.slice(0, limit) : rows;
   405	    for (const edge of edges) {
   406	      try { touchEdgeAccess(edge.id); } catch (e: unknown) {
   407	        console.warn(`[causal-edges] touchEdgeAccess failed for edge ${edge.id}: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`);
   408	      }
   409	    }
   410	    return createEdgeQueryResult(edges, limit, truncated);
   411	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   412	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   413	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getEdgesTo error: ${msg}`);
   414	    return createEdgeQueryResult([], limit, false);
   415	  }
   416	}
   417	
   418	function getAllEdges(limit: number = MAX_EDGES_LIMIT): EdgeQueryResult {
   419	  if (!db) return createEdgeQueryResult([], limit, false);
   420	
   421	  try {
   422	    const rows = (db.prepare(`
   423	      SELECT * FROM causal_edges
   424	      ORDER BY extracted_at DESC
   425	      LIMIT ?
   426	    `) as Database.Statement).all(limit + 1) as CausalEdge[];
   427	    const truncated = rows.length > limit;
   428	    const edges = truncated ? rows.slice(0, limit) : rows;
   429	    return createEdgeQueryResult(edges, limit, truncated);
   430	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   431	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   432	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getAllEdges error: ${msg}`);
   433	    return createEdgeQueryResult([], limit, false);
   434	  }
   435	}
   436	
   437	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   438	   6. GRAPH TRAVERSAL
   439	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   440	
   441	function getCausalChain(
   442	  rootId: string,
   443	  maxDepth: number = DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH,
   444	  direction: 'forward' | 'backward' = 'forward',
   445	  edgeLimit: number = MAX_EDGES_LIMIT,
   446	): CausalChainNode {
   447	  const root: CausalChainNode = {
   448	    id: rootId,
   449	    depth: 0,
   450	    relation: RELATION_TYPES.CAUSED,
   451	    strength: 1.0,
   452	    children: [],
   453	    truncated: false,
   454	    truncationLimit: null,
   455	  };
   456	
   457	  if (!db) return root;
   458	
   459	  function traverse(node: CausalChainNode, depth: number, path: Set<string>): void {
   460	    if (depth >= maxDepth) return;
   461	
   462	    const edges = direction === 'forward'
   463	      ? getEdgesFrom(node.id, edgeLimit)
   464	      : getEdgesTo(node.id, edgeLimit);
   465	
   466	    if (edges.truncated) {
   467	      root.truncated = true;
   468	      root.truncationLimit = edgeLimit;
   469	    }
   470	
   471	    for (const edge of edges) {
   472	      const nextId = direction === 'forward' ? edge.target_id : edge.source_id;
   473	      if (path.has(nextId)) continue;
   474	
   475	      // C138: apply cumulative path strength with relation weight multiplier,
   476	      // then clamp back to [0, 1].
   477	      const weight = RELATION_WEIGHTS[edge.relation] ?? 1.0;
   478	      const weightedStrength = clampStrength(node.strength * edge.strength * weight) ?? 0;
   479	
   480	      const child: CausalChainNode = {
   481	        id: nextId,
   482	        edgeId: edge.id,               // T202: preserve edge ID for unlink workflow
   483	        depth: depth + 1,
   484	        relation: edge.relation,
   485	        strength: weightedStrength,
   486	        children: [],
   487	      };
   488	
   489	      node.children.push(child);
   490	      const nextPath = new Set(path);
   491	      nextPath.add(nextId);
   492	      traverse(child, depth + 1, nextPath);
   493	    }
   494	  }
   495	
   496	  traverse(root, 0, new Set([rootId]));
   497	  return root;
   498	}
   499	
   500	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   501	   7. EDGE MANAGEMENT
   502	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   503	
   504	function updateEdge(
   505	  edgeId: number,
   506	  updates: { strength?: number; evidence?: string },
   507	  changedBy: string = 'manual',
   508	  reason: string | null = null,
   509	): boolean {
   510	  if (!db) return false;
   511	  const database = db;
   512	
   513	  try {
   514	    const parts: string[] = [];
   515	    const params: unknown[] = [];
   516	    let nextStrength: number | undefined;
   517	
   518	    if (updates.strength !== undefined) {
   519	      const clampedStrength = clampStrength(updates.strength);
   520	      if (clampedStrength === null) {
   521	        console.warn('[causal-edges] updateEdge rejected non-finite strength');
   522	        return false;
   523	      }
   524	      nextStrength = clampedStrength;
   525	      parts.push('strength = ?');
   526	      params.push(clampedStrength);
   527	    }
   528	    if (updates.evidence !== undefined) {
   529	      parts.push('evidence = ?');
   530	      params.push(updates.evidence);
   531	    }
   532	
   533	    if (parts.length === 0) return false;
   534	
   535	    params.push(edgeId);
   536	
   537	    // Wrap SELECT + UPDATE + logWeightChange in a transaction for atomicity
   538	    const changed = database.transaction(() => {
   539	      // T001d: Capture old strength for weight_history logging
   540	      let oldStrength: number | undefined;
   541	      if (updates.strength !== undefined) {
   542	        const existing = (database.prepare(
   543	          'SELECT strength FROM causal_edges WHERE id = ?'
   544	        ) as Database.Statement).get(edgeId) as { strength: number } | undefined;
   545	        oldStrength = existing?.strength;
   546	      }
   547	
   548	      // String interpolation constructs IN(?,?,?) placeholder list only —
   549	      // All user values are parameterized. Accepted exception per audit H-08.
   550	      const result = (database.prepare(
   551	        `UPDATE causal_edges SET ${parts.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?`
   552	      ) as Database.Statement).run(...params);
   553	
   554	      const changed = (result as { changes: number }).changes > 0;
   555	
   556	      // T001d: Log weight change to weight_history
   557	      if (changed && nextStrength !== undefined && isFiniteNumber(oldStrength)) {
   558	        if (oldStrength !== nextStrength) {
   559	          logWeightChange(edgeId, oldStrength, nextStrength, changedBy, reason);
   560	        }

 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	function computeGraphWalkMetrics(
   261	  db: Database.Database,
   262	  memoryIds: number[],
   263	): Map<number, GraphWalkMetrics> {
   264	  const uniqueIds = Array.from(new Set(memoryIds.filter((memoryId) => Number.isInteger(memoryId))));
   265	  const scores = new Map<number, GraphWalkMetrics>(
   266	    uniqueIds.map((memoryId) => [memoryId, { raw: 0, normalized: 0 }])
   267	  );
   268	
   269	  if (uniqueIds.length < 2) {
   270	    return scores;
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  const { adjacency } = buildAdjacencyList(db);
   274	  const graph = buildUndirectedAdjacency(adjacency);
   275	  const candidateSet = new Set(uniqueIds);
   276	  const candidateSpan = uniqueIds.length - 1;
   277	
   278	  for (const memoryId of uniqueIds) {
   279	    const directNeighbors = graph.get(memoryId) ?? new Set<number>();
   280	    let directHits = 0;
   281	    const secondHopHits = new Set<number>();
   282	
   283	    for (const neighbor of directNeighbors) {
   284	      if (neighbor !== memoryId && candidateSet.has(neighbor)) {
   285	        directHits += 1;
   286	      }
   287	
   288	      const neighborAdjacency = graph.get(neighbor);
   289	      if (!neighborAdjacency) {
   290	        continue;
   291	      }
   292	
   293	      for (const secondHop of neighborAdjacency) {
   294	        if (
   295	          secondHop === memoryId
   296	          || !candidateSet.has(secondHop)
   297	          || directNeighbors.has(secondHop)
   298	        ) {
   299	          continue;
   300	        }
   301	        secondHopHits.add(secondHop);
   302	      }
   303	    }
   304	
   305	    const weightedReach = directHits + (secondHopHits.size * GRAPH_WALK_SECOND_HOP_WEIGHT);
   306	    const normalizedReach = clamp(weightedReach / candidateSpan, 0, 1);
   307	    scores.set(memoryId, { raw: weightedReach, normalized: normalizedReach });
   308	  }
   309	
   310	  return scores;
   311	}
   312	
   313	function computeGraphWalkScores(
   314	  db: Database.Database,
   315	  memoryIds: number[],
   316	): Map<number, number> {
   317	  const metrics = computeGraphWalkMetrics(db, memoryIds);
   318	  return new Map(Array.from(metrics.entries()).map(([memoryId, value]) => [memoryId, value.normalized]));
   319	}
   320	
   321	type StronglyConnectedComponents = {
   322	  componentByNode: Map<number, number>;
   323	  components: number[][];
   324	};
   325	
   326	/**
   327	 * Collapse cyclic subgraphs into strongly connected components so we can
   328	 * compute longest-path depth on the resulting DAG without revisit drift.
   329	 */
   330	function buildStronglyConnectedComponents(
   331	  adjacency: Map<number, number[]>,
   332	  allNodes: Set<number>,
   333	): StronglyConnectedComponents {
   334	  const componentByNode = new Map<number, number>();
   335	  const components: number[][] = [];
   336	  const indices = new Map<number, number>();
   337	  const lowLinks = new Map<number, number>();
   338	  const stack: number[] = [];
   339	  const inStack = new Set<number>();
   340	  let nextIndex = 0;
   341	
   342	  function strongConnect(nodeId: number): void {
   343	    indices.set(nodeId, nextIndex);
   344	    lowLinks.set(nodeId, nextIndex);
   345	    nextIndex++;
   346	
   347	    stack.push(nodeId);
   348	    inStack.add(nodeId);
   349	
   350	    for (const neighbor of adjacency.get(nodeId) ?? []) {
   351	      if (!indices.has(neighbor)) {
   352	        strongConnect(neighbor);
   353	        lowLinks.set(
   354	          nodeId,
   355	          Math.min(lowLinks.get(nodeId) ?? 0, lowLinks.get(neighbor) ?? 0),
   356	        );
   357	      } else if (inStack.has(neighbor)) {
   358	        lowLinks.set(
   359	          nodeId,
   360	          Math.min(lowLinks.get(nodeId) ?? 0, indices.get(neighbor) ?? 0),
   361	        );
   362	      }
   363	    }
   364	
   365	    if ((lowLinks.get(nodeId) ?? -1) !== (indices.get(nodeId) ?? -2)) {
   366	      return;
   367	    }
   368	
   369	    const componentId = components.length;
   370	    const members: number[] = [];
   371	
   372	    while (stack.length > 0) {
   373	      const member = stack.pop();
   374	      if (member === undefined) break;
   375	
   376	      inStack.delete(member);
   377	      componentByNode.set(member, componentId);
   378	      members.push(member);
   379	
   380	      if (member === nodeId) break;
   381	    }
   382	
   383	    components.push(members);
   384	  }
   385	
   386	  for (const nodeId of allNodes) {
   387	    if (!indices.has(nodeId)) {
   388	      strongConnect(nodeId);
   389	    }
   390	  }
   391	
   392	  return { componentByNode, components };
   393	}
   394	
   395	/**
   396	 * Compute longest-path depths on the DAG formed by strongly connected
   397	 * components. Nodes within the same cycle share one bounded depth layer.
   398	 */
   399	function computeComponentDepths(
   400	  adjacency: Map<number, number[]>,
   401	  allNodes: Set<number>,
   402	): { depthByNode: Map<number, number>; maxDepth: number } {
   403	  const { componentByNode, components } = buildStronglyConnectedComponents(adjacency, allNodes);
   404	  const componentAdjacency = new Map<number, Set<number>>();
   405	  const componentInDegree = new Map<number, number>();
   406	
   407	  for (let componentId = 0; componentId < components.length; componentId++) {
   408	    componentAdjacency.set(componentId, new Set<number>());
   409	    componentInDegree.set(componentId, 0);
   410	  }
   411	
   412	  for (const [sourceId, neighbors] of adjacency.entries()) {
   413	    const sourceComponent = componentByNode.get(sourceId);
   414	    if (sourceComponent === undefined) continue;
   415	
   416	    const componentNeighbors = componentAdjacency.get(sourceComponent);
   417	    if (!componentNeighbors) continue;
   418	
   419	    for (const neighborId of neighbors) {
   420	      const targetComponent = componentByNode.get(neighborId);
   421	      if (targetComponent === undefined || targetComponent === sourceComponent || componentNeighbors.has(targetComponent)) {
   422	        continue;
   423	      }
   424	
   425	      componentNeighbors.add(targetComponent);
   426	      componentInDegree.set(targetComponent, (componentInDegree.get(targetComponent) ?? 0) + 1);
   427	    }
   428	  }
   429	
   430	  const remainingInDegree = new Map(componentInDegree);
   431	  const componentDepths = new Map<number, number>();
   432	  const queue: number[] = [];
   433	
   434	  for (let componentId = 0; componentId < components.length; componentId++) {
   435	    if ((remainingInDegree.get(componentId) ?? 0) === 0) {
   436	      componentDepths.set(componentId, 0);
   437	      queue.push(componentId);
   438	    }
   439	  }
   440	
   441	  let maxDepth = 0;
   442	  let queueIndex = 0;
   443	
   444	  while (queueIndex < queue.length) {
   445	    const componentId = queue[queueIndex++];
   446	    const componentDepth = componentDepths.get(componentId) ?? 0;
   447	
   448	    for (const neighborComponent of componentAdjacency.get(componentId) ?? []) {
   449	      const candidateDepth = componentDepth + 1;
   450	      if (candidateDepth > (componentDepths.get(neighborComponent) ?? 0)) {
   451	        componentDepths.set(neighborComponent, candidateDepth);
   452	        if (candidateDepth > maxDepth) {
   453	          maxDepth = candidateDepth;
   454	        }
   455	      }
   456	
   457	      const nextInDegree = (remainingInDegree.get(neighborComponent) ?? 0) - 1;
   458	      remainingInDegree.set(neighborComponent, nextInDegree);
   459	      if (nextInDegree === 0) {
   460	        queue.push(neighborComponent);
   461	      }
   462	    }
   463	  }
   464	
   465	  const depthByNode = new Map<number, number>();
   466	  for (const nodeId of allNodes) {
   467	    const componentId = componentByNode.get(nodeId);
   468	    if (componentId === undefined) continue;
   469	    depthByNode.set(nodeId, componentDepths.get(componentId) ?? 0);
   470	  }
   471	
   472	  return { depthByNode, maxDepth };
   473	}
   474	
   475	/**
   476	 * Batch-compute causal depth scores for a set of memory IDs.
   477	 * Uses the session cache to avoid redundant graph traversals within a session.
   478	 *
   479	 * Optimisation: when multiple IDs are requested, we build the adjacency list
   480	 * and compute component depths once, then cache all results.
   481	 *
   482	 * Shortcut edges should not collapse deeper causal chains to the nearest-root
   483	 * distance. We therefore compute longest-path depth on the DAG of strongly
   484	 * connected components, which also keeps cyclic subgraphs bounded.
   485	 */
   486	export function computeCausalDepthScores(db: Database.Database, memoryIds: number[]): Map<number, number> {
   487	  const results = new Map<number, number>();
   488	
   489	  // Separate cached from uncached
   490	  const uncached: number[] = [];
   491	  for (const memoryId of memoryIds) {
   492	    const cached = depthCache.get(memoryId);
   493	    if (cached !== undefined) {
   494	      results.set(memoryId, cached);
   495	    } else {
   496	      uncached.push(memoryId);
   497	    }
   498	  }
   499	
   500	  if (uncached.length === 0) return results;
   501	
   502	  // Build graph once for all uncached IDs
   503	  try {
   504	    const { adjacency, allNodes } = buildAdjacencyList(db);
   505	
   506	    if (allNodes.size === 0) {
   507	      for (const id of uncached) {
   508	        depthCache.set(id, 0);
   509	        results.set(id, 0);
   510	      }
   511	      return results;
   512	    }
   513	
   514	    const { depthByNode, maxDepth } = computeComponentDepths(adjacency, allNodes);
   515	
   516	    // Normalize and cache all uncached IDs
   517	    for (const id of uncached) {
   518	      let normalizedDepth = 0;
   519	      if (maxDepth > 0 && depthByNode.has(id)) {
   520	        normalizedDepth = (depthByNode.get(id) ?? 0) / maxDepth;
   521	      }
   522	      depthCache.set(id, normalizedDepth);
   523	      results.set(id, normalizedDepth);
   524	    }
   525	
   526	    // Enforce cache size bound after batch insert
   527	    enforceCacheBound(depthCache);
   528	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   529	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   530	    console.warn(`[graph-signals] computeCausalDepthScores failed: ${message}`);
   531	    // Fill uncached with 0 on error
   532	    for (const id of uncached) {
   533	      depthCache.set(id, 0);
   534	      results.set(id, 0);
   535	    }
   536	  }
   537	
   538	  return results;
   539	}
   540	
   541	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   542	// 3. COMBINED APPLICATION
   543	
   544	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   545	/**
   546	 * Clamp a value to [min, max].
   547	 */
   548	function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number): number {
   549	  return Math.min(max, Math.max(min, value));
   550	}
   551	
   552	/**
   553	 * Apply graph signal adjustments to scored search result rows.
   554	 *
   555	 * For each row:
   556	 * - Momentum bonus: clamp(momentum * 0.01, 0, 0.05) — rewards nodes gaining connections
   557	 * - Depth bonus: normalizedDepth * 0.05 — rewards structurally deep nodes
   558	 *
   559	 * Both bonuses are additive to the existing score.
   560	 *
   561	 * @param rows - Array of result rows with at least `id` and optional `score`.
   562	 * @param db   - Database instance for graph queries.
   563	 * @returns The same rows with adjusted scores.
   564	 */
   565	export function applyGraphSignals(
   566	  rows: Array<{ id: number; score?: number; [key: string]: unknown }>,
   567	  db: Database.Database,
   568	  options: { rolloutState?: GraphWalkRolloutState } = {},
   569	): Array<{ id: number; score?: number; [key: string]: unknown }> {
   570	  if (!rows || rows.length === 0) return rows;
   571	
   572	  try {
   573	    const ids = rows.map((row) => row.id);
   574	    const momentumScores = computeMomentumScores(db, ids);
   575	    const depthScores = computeCausalDepthScores(db, ids);
   576	    const graphWalkScores = computeGraphWalkMetrics(db, ids);
   577	    const rolloutState = options.rolloutState ?? 'bounded_runtime';
   578	
   579	    return rows.map((row) => {
   580	      const baseScore = typeof row.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.score) ? row.score : 0;
   581	      const momentum = momentumScores.get(row.id) ?? 0;
   582	      const depth = depthScores.get(row.id) ?? 0;
   583	      const graphWalk = graphWalkScores.get(row.id) ?? { raw: 0, normalized: 0 };
   584	
   585	      // Momentum bonus: up to +0.05
   586	      const momentumBonus = clamp(momentum * 0.01, 0, 0.05);
   587	      // Depth bonus: up to +0.05
   588	      const depthBonus = depth * 0.05;
   589	      // Graph-walk bonus: bounded local connectivity bonus across candidate rows
   590	      const unclampedGraphWalkBonus = graphWalk.normalized * STAGE2_GRAPH_BONUS_CAP;
   591	      const graphWalkBonus = rolloutState === 'bounded_runtime'
   592	        ? clampStage2GraphBonus(unclampedGraphWalkBonus)
   593	        : 0;
   594	      const capApplied = rolloutState === 'bounded_runtime'
   595	        && graphWalk.raw > 0
   596	        && graphWalk.normalized >= 1;
   597	
   598	      const adjustedScore = baseScore + momentumBonus + depthBonus + graphWalkBonus;
   599	      const existingContribution = (row.graphContribution && typeof row.graphContribution === 'object')
   600	        ? row.graphContribution as Record<string, unknown>
   601	        : {};
   602	
   603	      return {
   604	        ...row,
   605	        score: adjustedScore,
   606	        graphContribution: {
   607	          ...existingContribution,
   608	          raw: graphWalk.raw,
   609	          normalized: graphWalk.normalized,
   610	          appliedBonus: graphWalkBonus,
   611	          capApplied,
   612	          rolloutState,
   613	        },
   614	      };
   615	    });
   616	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   617	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   618	    console.warn(`[graph-signals] applyGraphSignals failed: ${message}`);
   619	    return rows;
   620	  }
   621	}
   622	
   623	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   624	// 4. TEST EXPORTS
   625	
   626	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   627	/**
   628	 * Internal functions exposed for unit testing.
   629	 * These are NOT part of the public API and may change without notice.
   630	 * Access only from `*.test.ts` / `*.vitest.ts` files.
   631	 *
   632	 * @internal
   633	 */
   634	export const __testables = {
   635	  getCurrentDegree,
   636	  getPastDegree,
   637	  buildAdjacencyList,
   638	  buildUndirectedAdjacency,
   639	  computeGraphWalkMetrics,
   640	  computeGraphWalkScores,
   641	  clamp,
   642	  momentumCache,
   643	  depthCache,
   644	};

     2	// MODULE: Community Detection
