     1	---
     2	title: Deep Research Dashboard Template
     3	description: Template for the auto-generated dashboard file regenerated after every iteration from JSONL + strategy data. Provides human-readable session overview and recovery surface.
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Deep Research Dashboard - Session Overview
     7	
     8	Auto-generated from JSONL state log and strategy file. Regenerated after every iteration evaluation. Never manually edited.
     9	
    10	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    11	## 1. OVERVIEW
    12	
    13	### Purpose
    14	
    15	Provides a compact, human-readable summary of the current research session. Serves three roles:
    16	1. **Observability** — quick status check during long autonomous runs
    17	2. **Recovery surface** — second recovery channel alongside JSONL after context compaction
    18	3. **Lifecycle decision** — in confirm mode, shown before `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, or `completed-continue`
    19	
    20	### Usage
    21	
    22	- **Location:** `{spec_folder}/research/deep-research-dashboard.md`
    23	- **Generated by:** Orchestrator (step_generate_dashboard in YAML workflow)
    24	- **Data sources:** `deep-research-state.jsonl` + `findings-registry.json` + `deep-research-strategy.md`
    25	- **Protection:** `auto-generated` — overwritten each iteration, never manually edited
    26	
    27	---
    28	
    29	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    30	<!-- ANCHOR:status -->
    31	## 2. STATUS
    32	- Topic: [from config]
    33	- Started: [from config.createdAt]
    34	- Status: [ITERATING | CONVERGING | STUCK_RECOVERY | COMPLETE]
    35	- Iteration: [N] of [maxIterations]
    36	- Session ID: [from config.lineage.sessionId]
    37	- Parent Session: [from config.lineage.parentSessionId or none]
    38	- Lifecycle Mode: [new | resume | restart | fork | completed-continue]
    39	- Generation: [from config.lineage.generation]
    40	
    41	---
    42	
    43	<!-- /ANCHOR:status -->
    44	<!-- ANCHOR:progress -->
    45	## 3. PROGRESS
    46	
    47	| # | Focus | Track | Ratio | Findings | Status |
    48	|---|-------|-------|-------|----------|--------|
    49	| [per iteration from JSONL] |
    50	
    51	- iterationsCompleted: [from findings registry metrics]
    52	- keyFindings: [from findings registry metrics]
    53	- openQuestions: [from findings registry metrics]
    54	- resolvedQuestions: [from findings registry metrics]
    55	
    56	---
    57	
    58	<!-- /ANCHOR:progress -->
    59	<!-- ANCHOR:questions -->
    60	## 4. QUESTIONS
    61	- Answered: [X/Y]
    62	- [x] Q1: [summary] (iteration N)
    63	- [ ] Q2: [remaining]
    64	
    65	---
    66	
    67	<!-- /ANCHOR:questions -->
    68	<!-- ANCHOR:trend -->
    69	## 5. TREND
    70	- Last 3 ratios: [0.XX -> 0.XX -> 0.XX] [improving | stable | declining]
    71	- Stuck count: [N]
    72	- Guard violations: [none | list]
    73	- convergenceScore: [from reducer metrics]
    74	- coverageBySources: [from reducer metrics]
    75	
    76	---
    77	
    78	<!-- /ANCHOR:trend -->
    79	<!-- ANCHOR:dead-ends -->

    85	<!-- /ANCHOR:dead-ends -->

     2	title: Deep Review Dashboard

     4	---
     5	
     6	# Deep Review Dashboard - Session Overview
     7	
     8	Auto-generated from JSONL state log and strategy file. Regenerated after every iteration evaluation. Never manually edited.
     9	
    10	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    11	## 1. OVERVIEW
    12	
    13	### Purpose
    14	
    15	Provides a compact, human-readable summary of the current review session. Serves three roles:
    16	1. **Observability** — quick status check during long autonomous review runs
    17	2. **Recovery surface** — second recovery channel alongside JSONL after context compaction
    18	3. **Resume decision** — in confirm mode, shown to user before resume/restart/fork choice
    19	
    20	### Usage
    21	
    22	- **Location:** `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-dashboard.md`
    23	- **Generated by:** Orchestrator (step_generate_dashboard in YAML workflow)
    24	- **Data sources:** `deep-review-state.jsonl` + `deep-review-strategy.md` + `deep-review-findings-registry.json`
    25	- **Protection:** `auto-generated` — overwritten each iteration, never manually edited
    26	
    27	---
    28	
    29	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    30	<!-- ANCHOR:status -->
    31	## 2. STATUS
    32	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
    33	- Target: [from config.reviewTarget]
    34	- Target Type: [from config.reviewTargetType]
    35	- Started: [from config.createdAt]
    36	- Session: [from config.sessionId] (generation [from config.generation], lineage [from config.lineageMode])
    37	- Status: [ITERATING | CONVERGING | STUCK_RECOVERY | COMPLETE]
    38	- Release Readiness: [in-progress | converged | release-blocking]
    39	- Iteration: [N] of [maxIterations]
    40	- Provisional Verdict: [PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL | PENDING]
    41	- hasAdvisories: [true | false]
    42	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
    43	
    44	---
    45	
    46	<!-- /ANCHOR:status -->
    47	<!-- ANCHOR:findings-summary -->
    48	## 3. FINDINGS SUMMARY
    49	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
    50	- **P0 (Critical):** [N] active, [N] new this iteration, [N] upgrades, [N] resolved
    51	- **P1 (Major):** [N] active, [N] new this iteration, [N] upgrades, [N] resolved
    52	- **P2 (Minor):** [N] active, [N] new this iteration, [N] upgrades, [N] resolved
    53	- **Repeated findings:** [N]
    54	- **Dimensions covered:** [correctness, security, traceability, maintainability]
    55	- **Convergence score:** [0.00-1.00]
    56	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
    57	
    58	---
    59	
    60	<!-- /ANCHOR:findings-summary -->
    61	<!-- ANCHOR:progress -->
    62	## 4. PROGRESS
    63	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
    64	
    65	| # | Focus | Files | Dimensions | New P0/P1/P2 | Ratio | Status |
    66	|---|-------|-------|------------|---------------|-------|--------|
    67	| [per iteration from JSONL] |
    68	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
    69	
    70	---
    71	
    72	<!-- /ANCHOR:progress -->
    73	<!-- ANCHOR:coverage -->
    74	## 5. COVERAGE
    75	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
    76	- Files reviewed: [X] / [Y] total
    77	- Dimensions complete: [X] / 4 total
    78	- Core protocols complete: [X] / [Y] required
    79	- Overlay protocols complete: [X] / [Y] applicable
    80	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
    81	
    82	---
    83	
    84	<!-- /ANCHOR:coverage -->
    85	<!-- ANCHOR:trend -->
    86	## 6. TREND
    87	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
    88	- Severity trend (last 3): [P0:N P1:N P2:N -> P0:N P1:N P2:N -> P0:N P1:N P2:N]
    89	- New findings trend (last 3): [N -> N -> N] [increasing | stable | decreasing]
    90	- Traceability trend (last 3): [pass/partial/fail counts -> ...]
    91	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
    92	
    93	---
    94	
    95	<!-- /ANCHOR:trend -->
    96	<!-- ANCHOR:resolved -->
    97	## 7. RESOLVED / RULED OUT
    98	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
    99	- **Disproved findings:** [findings investigated and determined to be false positives]
   100	- **Dead-end review paths:** [review approaches that yielded no actionable results]
   101	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
   102	
   103	---
   104	
   105	<!-- /ANCHOR:resolved -->
   106	<!-- ANCHOR:next-focus -->
   107	## 8. NEXT FOCUS
   108	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
   109	[from strategy.md "Next Focus" section — next dimension, file set, or review lens]
   110	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
   111	
   112	---

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:335:Scoring: Rank by newInfoRatio, prune below median when wave support is enabled
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:347:1. Rank all wave iterations by `newInfoRatio`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:348:2. Compute wave median: `median([i.newInfoRatio for i in wave_iterations])`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:349:3. **Prune**: Questions with newInfoRatio below median are deprioritized when wave support is enabled
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:355:When any single iteration in a wave achieves `newInfoRatio > 2x wave_average`:

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:129:{"type":"iteration","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"Initial broad survey","findingsCount":5,"newInfoRatio":1.0,"keyQuestions":["What causes latency?","Where are bottlenecks?"],"answeredQuestions":["What causes latency?"],"timestamp":"2026-03-18T10:05:00Z","durationMs":45000}
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:136:| status | string | iteration only | complete, timeout, error, stuck, insight, thought |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:139:| newInfoRatio | number | iteration only | Fraction of new vs redundant info (0.0-1.0) |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:141:| answeredQuestions | string[] | iteration only | Questions fully answered this iteration |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:145:| sourcesQueried | string[] | No | iteration only | URLs, file paths, or memory anchors consulted during the iteration |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:147:| convergenceSignals | object | No | Composite convergence signal values (see below) |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:149:| noveltyJustification | string | No | Human-readable explanation of what newInfoRatio represents (see below) |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:150:| focusTrack | string | No | Post-hoc grouping label, e.g. `"browser-support"`. Not used for orchestration |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:157:{"type":"iteration","run":5,...,"convergenceSignals":{"rollingAvg":0.12,"madScore":0.08,"entropyCoverage":0.71,"compositeStop":0.42}}
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:162:| rollingAvg | number | Rolling average of last 3 newInfoRatio values |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:174:{"type":"iteration","run":3,"status":"complete","focus":"Connection pooling","findingsCount":3,"newInfoRatio":0.4,"ruledOut":[{"approach":"HTTP/3 multiplexing","reason":"No server-side support in target environment","evidence":"docs.example.com/protocols#supported"}],"keyQuestions":["What causes latency?"],"answeredQuestions":[],"timestamp":"2026-03-18T10:15:00Z","durationMs":52000}
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:187:The optional `noveltyJustification` field provides a human-readable breakdown of what the `newInfoRatio` represents:
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:190:{"type":"iteration","run":3,"status":"complete","focus":"Reconnection strategies","findingsCount":4,"newInfoRatio":0.7,"noveltyJustification":"2 new findings on reconnection backoff, 1 refinement of prior keepalive finding","keyQuestions":["How to handle reconnection?"],"answeredQuestions":[],"timestamp":"2026-03-18T10:15:00Z","durationMs":48000}
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:197:The optional `focusTrack` field assigns a grouping label to an iteration for post-hoc analysis:
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:200:{"type":"iteration","run":4,...,"focusTrack":"browser-support",...}
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:215:In iteration file findings sections, annotate inline: `[Finding text] (sourceStrength: primary)`. In JSONL records, source strength is tracked implicitly through the `newInfoRatio` -- tentative findings contribute less to the ratio. The convergence algorithm excludes tentative-only findings when computing `entropyCoverage`.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:219:The iteration `status` field supports the following values:
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:227:| `insight` | Low `newInfoRatio` but contains an important conceptual breakthrough worth preserving |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:230:The `insight` status prevents premature convergence when a conceptually significant iteration would otherwise trigger the stuck counter. The `thought` status marks planning or meta-reasoning iterations that should not affect convergence signals.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:250:| missing_newInfoRatio | parser | active | Missing ratio defaulted to 0.0 | iteration, timestamp |

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:349:**Limitations**: Reconstructed state lacks `durationMs`, exact `timestamp`, and `findingsCount`. The `status: "reconstructed"` flag distinguishes recovered records from originals. The convergence algorithm treats reconstructed records identically to complete records for signal computation.

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:193:### newInfoRatio (0.0 to 1.0)
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:207:When computing newInfoRatio, consider:
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:212:Formula: `newInfoRatio = (fully_new + 0.5 * partially_new) / total_findings`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:224:    if i.status == "thought":
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:228:    if i.status == "insight":
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:230:    if i.newInfoRatio < config.convergenceThreshold or i.status == "stuck":
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:238:- `newInfoRatio < config.convergenceThreshold` (below the configured no-progress threshold)
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:239:- OR `status == "stuck"` (agent self-reported as stuck)
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:247:Computed by the orchestrator from all historical newInfoRatio values.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:251:  ratios = [i.newInfoRatio for i in iterations]
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:258:The MAD noise floor represents the minimum change needed to distinguish signal from noise. When the latest newInfoRatio falls at or below the noise floor, the MAD signal votes "stop".
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:294:   - `status ?? "complete"`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:295:   - `newInfoRatio ?? 0.0`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:383:**Trigger**: Mid-range plateau (`newInfoRatio` between the configured threshold and 0.20 for 3+ iterations, not fully stuck).
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:384:**Action**: Synthesize the two highest-newInfoRatio iterations into a new composite question.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:385:- Read the top-2 iterations by newInfoRatio
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:392:**Action**: Re-read iterations with newInfoRatio below 0.20 and extract buried insights.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:413:  recentRatios = [i.newInfoRatio for i in stuckIterations[-3:]]
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:423:- If `newInfoRatio > config.convergenceThreshold`: Recovery successful. Reset stuck count. Continue.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:424:- If `newInfoRatio <= config.convergenceThreshold`: Recovery failed. Exit to synthesis with gaps documented.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:455:When 2 consecutive iterations on the same focus yield newInfoRatio < 0.10:
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:465:- Write reconstructed JSONL with `status: "reconstructed"`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:493:After each iteration, compute the noise floor from all historical newInfoRatio values to validate the latest ratio.

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:585:### newInfoRatio Reporting Error
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:586:If the agent fails to report `newInfoRatio` in its JSONL entry:
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:588:- Log a warning in the JSONL: `{"type":"event","event":"missing_newInfoRatio","iteration":N}`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:633:The review-mode equivalent of `newInfoRatio`. Computed by the @deep-review agent at the end of each iteration.

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:2:title: "DR-029 -- Insight status prevents false stuck detection"
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:3:description: "Verify that an iteration with status insight does not increment stuck count despite low newInfoRatio."
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:6:# DR-029 -- Insight status prevents false stuck detection
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:14:This scenario validates insight status handling for `DR-029`. The objective is to verify that an iteration with status "insight" (low newInfoRatio but containing an important conceptual breakthrough) does NOT increment the stuck counter.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:18:Without insight-status protection, a eureka moment that reshapes the research direction but yields little raw data would be misclassified as stuck, potentially triggering premature recovery or convergence exit.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:26:- Objective: Verify that an iteration with status "insight" and low newInfoRatio does NOT increment stuck count.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:28:- Orchestrator prompt: Validate the insight-status contract for sk-deep-research. Confirm that state_format.md defines "insight" as a valid iteration status, that convergence.md documents how stuckCount is computed, and that insight iterations are excluded from stuck counting, then return a concise operator-facing verdict.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:29:- Expected execution process: Inspect the state format reference for the insight status definition, then the convergence reference for stuckCount computation rules, then the SKILL.md for the iteration status summary.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:31:- Expected signals: Iteration with status="insight" and low newInfoRatio, stuck_count NOT incremented.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:32:- Pass/fail posture: PASS if state_format.md defines "insight" status as preventing stuck counting AND convergence.md confirms stuckCount excludes insight iterations; FAIL if insight iterations are counted toward stuck or the documentation is contradictory.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/029-insight-status-prevents-false-stuck.md:47:| DR-029 | Insight status prevents false stuck detection | Verify that an iteration with status insight does not increment stuck count despite low newInfoRatio. | Validate the insight-status contract for sk-deep-research. .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:2:title: "DR-030 -- Thought status handling in convergence"
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:6:# DR-030 -- Thought status handling in convergence
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:14:This scenario validates thought status convergence handling for `DR-030`. The objective is to verify that "thought" iterations (analytical-only, no evidence gathering) are handled correctly in convergence: they do not count as stuck and do not count toward the rolling average.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:28:- Orchestrator prompt: Validate the thought-status convergence contract for sk-deep-research. Confirm that state_format.md defines "thought" as a valid iteration status marking analytical-only iterations, that convergence.md documents how thought iterations are excluded from stuck counting and the rolling average, and that SKILL.md lists it in the status taxonomy, then return a concise operator-facing verdict.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:29:- Expected execution process: Inspect the state format reference for the thought status definition, then the convergence reference for its handling in the shouldContinue algorithm, then the SKILL.md for the iteration status summary.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:30:- Desired user-facing outcome: The user understands that a thought iteration is recognized as deliberate meta-reasoning and is excluded from both stuck counting and the rolling newInfoRatio average.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:31:- Expected signals: Iteration with status="thought", convergence treats it appropriately (does not count as stuck, does not count toward rolling average).
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:32:- Pass/fail posture: PASS if state_format.md defines "thought" status AND convergence.md confirms thought iterations are excluded from stuck counting and rolling average; FAIL if thought iterations affect convergence signals or the documentation is contradictory.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/030-thought-status-convergence-handling.md:47:| DR-030 | Thought status handling in convergence | Verify that thought iterations are handled correctly in convergence and do not count as stuck or toward rolling average. | Validate the thought-status convergence contract for sk-deep-research. Confirm that state_format.md defines "thought" as a valid iteration status marking analytical-only iterations, that convergence.md documents how thought iterations are excluded from stuck counting and the rolling average, and that SKILL.md lists it in the status taxonomy, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: rg -n 'thought' .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md` -> 2. `bash: rg -n 'thought\|rolling.average\|stuckCount' .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md` -> 3. `bash: rg -n 'thought\|insight\|stuck' .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md` -> 4. `bash: rg -n 'stuck_count\|thought' .opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml` | Iteration with status="thought", convergence treats it appropriately (does not count as stuck, does not count toward rolling average). | Capture the state_format.md thought status definition, the convergence.md handling rules for thought iterations, and the SKILL.md iteration status taxonomy. | PASS if state_format.md defines "thought" status AND convergence.md confirms thought iterations are excluded from stuck counting and rolling average; FAIL if thought iterations affect convergence signals or the documentation is contradictory. | Privilege state_format.md for the status taxonomy and convergence.md for the algorithm; use SKILL.md and YAML only as secondary confirmation. |

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/025-novelty-justification-in-jsonl.md:14:This scenario validates that novelty justification is present in JSONL iteration records for `DR-025`. The objective is to verify that every iteration record includes both `newInfoRatio` and a human-readable `noveltyJustification` field.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/025-novelty-justification-in-jsonl.md:18:A bare `newInfoRatio` number (e.g., 0.70) is opaque without context. The `noveltyJustification` field explains what the ratio means in concrete terms, enabling post-hoc analysis and helping operators and users understand whether the research is genuinely progressing or just re-covering familiar ground.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/025-novelty-justification-in-jsonl.md:26:- Objective: Verify every iteration JSONL record includes noveltyJustification alongside newInfoRatio.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/025-novelty-justification-in-jsonl.md:28:- Orchestrator prompt: Validate the novelty justification contract for sk-deep-research. Confirm that the JSONL iteration record schema requires both `newInfoRatio` and `noveltyJustification`, that the justification is a human-readable sentence, and that ALWAYS rule 11 and the agent Step 6 mandate their inclusion. Return a concise operator-facing verdict.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/025-novelty-justification-in-jsonl.md:30:- Desired user-facing outcome: The user can inspect any JSONL iteration record and find a plain-language explanation of what the newInfoRatio represents.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/025-novelty-justification-in-jsonl.md:31:- Expected signals: JSONL iteration records contain both `newInfoRatio` (number, 0.0-1.0) and `noveltyJustification` (string, human-readable sentence); the justification field is listed as required in v1.1.0 agent instructions; ALWAYS rule 11 mandates both fields.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/025-novelty-justification-in-jsonl.md:47:| DR-025 | Novelty justification present in JSONL | Verify every iteration JSONL record includes noveltyJustification alongside newInfoRatio. | Validate the novelty justification contract for sk-deep-research. Return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: rg -n 'noveltyJustification\|Novelty Justification' .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md` -> 2. `bash: rg -n 'rule.*11\|novelty.*justification\|newInfoRatio.*novelty' .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md` -> 3. `bash: rg -n 'noveltyJustification\|Required fields' .opencode/agent/deep-research.md` | JSONL record has both `newInfoRatio` and `noveltyJustification` fields; justification is a human-readable sentence (e.g., "2 new findings on reconnection backoff, 1 refinement of prior keepalive finding"); field is listed as required in v1.1.0 agent instructions (Step 6); ALWAYS rule 11 mandates both. | Capture the state format field definition, SKILL.md ALWAYS rule 11 text, and agent Step 6 required fields list with the example JSONL record. | PASS if the field schema, ALWAYS rule 11, and agent Step 6 all consistently require both fields and the justification is a human-readable sentence; FAIL if any source omits the requirement or defines the field as optional without enforcement. | Privilege the SKILL.md ALWAYS rules as the normative contract; use state_format.md for schema details and agent files for implementation enforcement. Note: state_format.md marks the field as `No` (not required) in the schema table but SKILL.md ALWAYS rule 11 and agent v1.1.0 instructions override this to required. |

   327	
   328	/** Batch insert edges (deletes edges from the source nodes first) */
   329	export function replaceEdges(sourceIds: string[], edges: CodeEdge[]): void {
   330	  const d = getDb();
   331	
   332	  const insert = d.prepare(`
   333	    INSERT INTO code_edges (source_id, target_id, edge_type, weight, metadata)
   334	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   335	  `);
   336	
   337	  const tx = d.transaction(() => {
   338	    if (sourceIds.length > 0) {
   339	      const placeholders = sourceIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
   340	      d.prepare(`DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...sourceIds);
   341	    }
   342	
   343	    for (const e of edges) {
   344	      insert.run(e.sourceId, e.targetId, e.edgeType, e.weight, e.metadata ? JSON.stringify(e.metadata) : null);
   345	    }
   346	  });
   347	  tx();
   348	}
   349	
   350	/** Check if a file needs re-indexing based on stored mtime */
   351	export function isFileStale(filePath: string): boolean {
   352	  const d = getDb();
   353	  const row = d.prepare('SELECT file_mtime_ms FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as { file_mtime_ms: number } | undefined;
   354	  if (!row) return true;
   355	  const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
   356	  if (currentMtimeMs === null) return true;
   357	  return row.file_mtime_ms !== currentMtimeMs;
   358	}
   359	
   360	/** Batch stale check for a set of file paths */
   361	export function ensureFreshFiles(filePaths: string[]): { stale: string[]; fresh: string[] } {
   362	  const uniquePaths = [...new Set(filePaths)];
   363	  if (uniquePaths.length === 0) {
   364	    return { stale: [], fresh: [] };
   365	  }
   366	
   367	  const d = getDb();
   368	  const placeholders = uniquePaths.map(() => '?').join(',');
   369	  const rows = d.prepare(`
   370	    SELECT file_path, file_mtime_ms
   371	    FROM code_files
   372	    WHERE file_path IN (${placeholders})
   373	  `).all(...uniquePaths) as Array<{ file_path: string; file_mtime_ms: number }>;
   374	  const storedMtimes = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.file_path, row.file_mtime_ms]));
   375	
   376	  const stale: string[] = [];
   377	  const fresh: string[] = [];
   378	
   379	  for (const filePath of uniquePaths) {
   380	    const storedMtimeMs = storedMtimes.get(filePath);
   381	    const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
   382	    if (storedMtimeMs === undefined || currentMtimeMs === null || storedMtimeMs !== currentMtimeMs) {
   383	      stale.push(filePath);
   384	      continue;
   385	    }
   386	    fresh.push(filePath);
   387	  }
   388	
   389	  return { stale, fresh };
   390	}
   391	
   392	/** Get all tracked file paths from the graph database */
   393	export function getTrackedFiles(): string[] {
   394	  const d = getDb();
   395	  const rows = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files').all() as Array<{ file_path: string }>;
   396	  return rows.map((row) => row.file_path);
   397	}
   398	
   399	/** Remove a file and its nodes/edges from the graph */
   400	export function removeFile(filePath: string): void {
   401	  const d = getDb();
   402	  const file = d.prepare('SELECT id FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as { id: number } | undefined;
   403	  if (!file) return;
   404	  // CASCADE will handle nodes; edges need manual cleanup
   405	  const nodeIds = d.prepare('SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').all(file.id) as { symbol_id: string }[];
   406	  if (nodeIds.length > 0) {
   407	    const placeholders = nodeIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
   408	    const ids = nodeIds.map(n => n.symbol_id);
   409	    d.prepare(`DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...ids, ...ids);
   410	  }
   411	  d.prepare('DELETE FROM code_files WHERE id = ?').run(file.id);
   412	}
   413	
   414	/** Query: get file outline (nodes sorted by line) */
   415	export function queryOutline(filePath: string): CodeNode[] {
   416	  const d = getDb();
   417	  const rows = d.prepare(`
   418	    SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE file_path = ? ORDER BY start_line
   419	  `).all(filePath) as Record<string, unknown>[];
   420	
   421	  return rows.map(rowToNode);
   422	}
   423	
   424	/** Query: startup-friendly highlights — most-called project symbols (incoming calls). */
   425	export function queryStartupHighlights(limit: number = 5): StartupHighlight[] {
   426	  const d = getDb();
   427	  const safeLimit = Number.isFinite(limit) ? Math.max(1, Math.min(Math.trunc(limit), 20)) : 5;
   428	  const rows = d.prepare(`
   429	    WITH filtered_nodes AS (
   430	      SELECT n.symbol_id, n.name, n.kind, n.file_path, n.start_line
   431	      FROM code_nodes n
   432	      WHERE n.kind IN ('class', 'function', 'method', 'interface', 'type_alias', 'module')
   433	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/site-packages/%'
   434	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/node_modules/%'
   435	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/.venv/%'
   436	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/vendor/%'
   437	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/dist/%'
   438	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/build/%'
   439	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/__pycache__/%'
   440	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/tests/%'
   441	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/test_%'
   442	        AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%__tests__%'
   443	    ),
   444	    aggregated AS (
   445	      SELECT
   446	        fn.name as name,
   447	        fn.kind as kind,
   448	        fn.file_path as file_path,
   449	        MIN(fn.start_line) as start_line,
   450	        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN UPPER(e.edge_type) = 'CALLS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) as call_count
   451	      FROM filtered_nodes fn
   452	      LEFT JOIN code_edges e
   453	        ON e.target_id = fn.symbol_id
   454	      GROUP BY fn.name, fn.kind, fn.file_path
   455	    ),
   456	    ranked AS (
   457	      SELECT
   458	        name,
   459	        kind,
   460	        file_path,
   461	        call_count,
   462	        start_line,
   463	        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
   464	          PARTITION BY file_path
   465	          ORDER BY call_count DESC, start_line ASC
   466	        ) as file_rank
   467	      FROM aggregated
   468	    )
   469	    SELECT
   470	      name,
   471	      kind,
   472	      file_path,
   473	      call_count
   474	    FROM ranked
   475	    WHERE file_rank <= 2
   476	    ORDER BY call_count DESC, start_line ASC
   477	    LIMIT ?
   478	  `).all(safeLimit) as Array<{
   479	    name: string;
   480	    kind: string;
   481	    file_path: string;
   482	    call_count: number;
   483	  }>;
   484	
   485	  return rows.map((row) => ({
   486	    name: row.name,
   487	    kind: row.kind,
   488	    filePath: row.file_path,
   489	    callCount: row.call_count,
   490	  }));
   491	}
   492	
   493	/** Query: get edges from a symbol */
   494	export function queryEdgesFrom(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): { edge: CodeEdge; targetNode: CodeNode | null }[] {
   495	  const d = getDb();
   496	  let sql = 'SELECT * FROM code_edges WHERE source_id = ?';
   497	  const params: unknown[] = [symbolId];
   498	  if (edgeType) {
   499	    sql += ' AND edge_type = ?';
   500	    params.push(edgeType);
   501	  }
   502	
   503	  const edges = d.prepare(sql).all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[];
   504	  return edges.map(e => {
   505	    const edge = rowToEdge(e);
   506	    const targetRow = d.prepare('SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ?').get(edge.targetId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
   507	    return { edge, targetNode: targetRow ? rowToNode(targetRow) : null };
   508	  });
   509	}
   510	
   511	/** Query: get edges to a symbol */
   512	export function queryEdgesTo(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): { edge: CodeEdge; sourceNode: CodeNode | null }[] {
   513	  const d = getDb();
   514	  let sql = 'SELECT * FROM code_edges WHERE target_id = ?';
   515	  const params: unknown[] = [symbolId];
   516	  if (edgeType) {
   517	    sql += ' AND edge_type = ?';
   518	    params.push(edgeType);
   519	  }
   520	
   521	  const edges = d.prepare(sql).all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[];
   522	  return edges.map(e => {
   523	    const edge = rowToEdge(e);
   524	    const sourceRow = d.prepare('SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ?').get(edge.sourceId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
   525	    return { edge, sourceNode: sourceRow ? rowToNode(sourceRow) : null };
   526	  });
   527	}
   528	
   529	export function resolveSubjectFilePath(subject: string): string | null {
   530	  const d = getDb();
   531	
   532	  const directFile = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
   533	  if (directFile) return directFile.file_path;
   534	
   535	  const byId = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
   536	  if (byId) return byId.file_path;
   537	
   538	  const byFq = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE fq_name = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
   539	  if (byFq) return byFq.file_path;
   540	
   541	  const byName = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE name = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
   542	  if (byName) return byName.file_path;
   543	
   544	  return null;
   545	}
   546	
   547	export function queryFileImportDependents(): FileImportDependent[] {
   548	  const d = getDb();
   549	  const rows = d.prepare(`
   550	    SELECT DISTINCT
   551	      target.file_path AS imported_file_path,
   552	      source.file_path AS importer_file_path
   553	    FROM code_edges edge
   554	    INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
   555	    INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
   556	    WHERE UPPER(edge.edge_type) = 'IMPORTS'
   557	      AND source.file_path != target.file_path
   558	  `).all() as Array<{
   559	    imported_file_path: string;
   560	    importer_file_path: string;
   561	  }>;
   562	
   563	  return rows.map((row) => ({
   564	    importedFilePath: row.imported_file_path,
   565	    importerFilePath: row.importer_file_path,
   566	  }));
   567	}
   568	
   569	export function queryFileDegrees(filePaths: string[]): FileDegree[] {
   570	  const uniquePaths = [...new Set(filePaths.filter((filePath) => typeof filePath === 'string' && filePath.length > 0))];
   571	  if (uniquePaths.length === 0) {
   572	    return [];
   573	  }
   574	
   575	  const d = getDb();
   576	  const placeholders = uniquePaths.map(() => '?').join(',');
   577	  const rows = d.prepare(`
   578	    SELECT
   579	      node_file_path,
   580	      COUNT(DISTINCT connected_file_path) AS degree
   581	    FROM (
   582	      SELECT
   583	        source.file_path AS node_file_path,
   584	        target.file_path AS connected_file_path
   585	      FROM code_edges edge
   586	      INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
   587	      INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
   588	      WHERE source.file_path IN (${placeholders})
   589	        AND source.file_path != target.file_path
   590	
   591	      UNION ALL
   592	
   593	      SELECT
   594	        target.file_path AS node_file_path,
   595	        source.file_path AS connected_file_path
   596	      FROM code_edges edge
   597	      INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
   598	      INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
   599	      WHERE target.file_path IN (${placeholders})
   600	        AND source.file_path != target.file_path
   601	    )
   602	    GROUP BY node_file_path
   603	  `).all(...uniquePaths, ...uniquePaths) as Array<{
   604	    node_file_path: string;
   605	    degree: number;
   606	  }>;
   607	
   608	  const degreeByFile = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.node_file_path, row.degree]));
   609	  return uniquePaths.map((filePath) => ({
   610	    filePath,
   611	    degree: degreeByFile.get(filePath) ?? 0,
   612	  }));
   613	}
   614	
   615	/** Get graph statistics */
   616	export function getStats(): {
   617	  totalFiles: number; totalNodes: number; totalEdges: number;
   618	  nodesByKind: Record<string, number>; edgesByType: Record<string, number>;
   619	  parseHealthSummary: Record<string, number>;
   620	  lastScanTimestamp: string | null;
   621	  lastGitHead: string | null;
   622	  dbFileSize: number | null;
   623	  schemaVersion: number;
   624	} {
   625	  const d = getDb();
   626	  const totalFiles = (d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM code_files').get() as { c: number }).c;
   627	  const totalNodes = (d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM code_nodes').get() as { c: number }).c;
   628	  const totalEdges = (d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM code_edges').get() as { c: number }).c;
   629	
   630	  const nodesByKind: Record<string, number> = {};
   631	  const kindRows = d.prepare('SELECT kind, COUNT(*) as c FROM code_nodes GROUP BY kind').all() as { kind: string; c: number }[];
   632	  for (const r of kindRows) nodesByKind[r.kind] = r.c;
   633	
   634	  const edgesByType: Record<string, number> = {};
   635	  const typeRows = d.prepare('SELECT edge_type, COUNT(*) as c FROM code_edges GROUP BY edge_type').all() as { edge_type: string; c: number }[];
   636	  for (const r of typeRows) edgesByType[r.edge_type] = r.c;
   637	
   638	  const parseHealthSummary: Record<string, number> = {};
   639	  const healthRows = d.prepare('SELECT parse_health, COUNT(*) as c FROM code_files GROUP BY parse_health').all() as { parse_health: string; c: number }[];
   640	  for (const r of healthRows) parseHealthSummary[r.parse_health] = r.c;
   641	
   642	  // Last scan timestamp
   643	  const lastScan = d.prepare('SELECT MAX(indexed_at) as last FROM code_files').get() as { last: string | null } | undefined;
   644	  const lastScanTimestamp = lastScan?.last ?? null;
   645	  const lastGitHead = getLastGitHead();
   646	
   647	  // DB file size
   648	  let dbFileSize: number | null = null;
   649	  if (dbPath) {
   650	    try { dbFileSize = statSync(dbPath).size; } catch { /* file may not exist yet */ }
   651	  }
   652	
   653	  return {
   654	    totalFiles, totalNodes, totalEdges, nodesByKind, edgesByType, parseHealthSummary,
   655	    lastScanTimestamp, lastGitHead, dbFileSize, schemaVersion: SCHEMA_VERSION,
   656	  };
   657	}
   658	
   659	/** Remove orphaned nodes whose files no longer exist in code_files */
   660	export function cleanupOrphans(): number {
   661	  const d = getDb();
   662	  // Find node file_ids not in code_files
   663	  const orphanedNodes = d.prepare(`
   664	    DELETE FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM code_files)
   665	  `).run();
   666	
   667	  // Find edges referencing non-existent nodes
   668	  const orphanedEdges = d.prepare(`
   669	    DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE
   670	      source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR
   671	      target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
   672	  `).run();
   673	
   674	  return orphanedNodes.changes + orphanedEdges.changes;
   675	}
   676	
   677	/** Convert DB row to CodeNode */
   678	function rowToNode(r: Record<string, unknown>): CodeNode {
   679	  return {
   680	    symbolId: r.symbol_id as string,
   681	    filePath: r.file_path as string,
   682	    fqName: r.fq_name as string,
   683	    kind: r.kind as CodeNode['kind'],
   684	    name: r.name as string,
   685	    startLine: r.start_line as number,
   686	    endLine: r.end_line as number,
   687	    startColumn: r.start_column as number ?? 0,
   688	    endColumn: r.end_column as number ?? 0,
   689	    language: r.language as CodeNode['language'],
   690	    signature: r.signature as string | undefined,
   691	    docstring: r.docstring as string | undefined,
   692	    contentHash: r.content_hash as string,
   693	  };
   694	}
   695	
   696	/** Convert DB row to CodeEdge */
   697	function rowToEdge(r: Record<string, unknown>): CodeEdge {
   698	  return {
   699	    sourceId: r.source_id as string,
   700	    targetId: r.target_id as string,
   701	    edgeType: r.edge_type as CodeEdge['edgeType'],
   702	    weight: r.weight as number,
   703	    metadata: r.metadata ? JSON.parse(r.metadata as string) : undefined,
   704	  };
   705	}
   706	
   707	/** Compute token usage ratio (completion / total) for budget allocator consumption */
   708	export function getTokenUsageRatio(
   709	  sessionMetrics: { estimatedPromptTokens: number; estimatedCompletionTokens: number } | null,
   710	): number {
   711	  if (!sessionMetrics) return 0;
   712	  const total = sessionMetrics.estimatedPromptTokens + sessionMetrics.estimatedCompletionTokens;
   713	  if (total === 0) return 0;
   714	  return sessionMetrics.estimatedCompletionTokens / total;
   715	}

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md | sed -n '120,220p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   120	### Line 1: Config Record
   121	
   122	```json
   123	{"type":"config","topic":"API response time optimization","maxIterations":10,"convergenceThreshold":0.05,"createdAt":"2026-03-18T10:00:00Z","specFolder":"028-auto-deep-research"}
   124	```
   125	
   126	### Iteration Records
   127	
   128	```json
   129	{"type":"iteration","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"Initial broad survey","findingsCount":5,"newInfoRatio":1.0,"keyQuestions":["What causes latency?","Where are bottlenecks?"],"answeredQuestions":["What causes latency?"],"timestamp":"2026-03-18T10:05:00Z","durationMs":45000}
   130	```
   131	
   132	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   133	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
   134	| type | "config", "iteration", or "event" | Yes | Record type discriminator |
   135	| run | number | iteration only | 1-indexed iteration number |
   136	| status | string | iteration only | complete, timeout, error, stuck, insight, thought |
   137	| focus | string | iteration only | What this iteration investigated |
   138	| findingsCount | number | iteration only | Number of distinct findings |
   139	| newInfoRatio | number | iteration only | Fraction of new vs redundant info (0.0-1.0) |
   140	| keyQuestions | string[] | iteration only | Questions addressed this iteration |
   141	| answeredQuestions | string[] | iteration only | Questions fully answered this iteration |
   142	| timestamp | ISO 8601 | Yes | Record creation time |
   143	| durationMs | number | iteration only | Iteration execution time in milliseconds |
   144	| toolsUsed | string[] | No | iteration only | High-level tool names used during the iteration |
   145	| sourcesQueried | string[] | No | iteration only | URLs, file paths, or memory anchors consulted during the iteration |
   146	| segment | number | No | Segment number (default: 1). Groups iterations into logical phases |
   147	| convergenceSignals | object | No | Composite convergence signal values (see below) |
   148	| ruledOut | array | No | Approaches eliminated this iteration (see Negative Knowledge below) |
   149	| noveltyJustification | string | No | Human-readable explanation of what newInfoRatio represents (see below) |
   150	| focusTrack | string | No | Post-hoc grouping label, e.g. `"browser-support"`. Not used for orchestration |
   151	
   152	### Convergence Signal Fields
   153	
   154	When the composite convergence algorithm runs (see convergence.md), signal values are recorded in the iteration record:
   155	
   156	```json
   157	{"type":"iteration","run":5,...,"convergenceSignals":{"rollingAvg":0.12,"madScore":0.08,"entropyCoverage":0.71,"compositeStop":0.42}}
   158	```
   159	
   160	| Field | Type | Description |
   161	|-------|------|-------------|
   162	| rollingAvg | number | Rolling average of last 3 newInfoRatio values |
   163	| madScore | number | MAD-based noise floor value |
   164	| entropyCoverage | number | Question coverage ratio (0.0-1.0) |
   165	| compositeStop | number | Weighted stop score (stop if > 0.60) |
   166	
   167	All fields are optional. Omitted when fewer than 3 iterations exist (insufficient data for composite). Backward compatible: old records without this field parse normally.
   168	
   169	### Negative Knowledge (ruledOut)
   170	
   171	Iteration records may include a `ruledOut` array documenting approaches that were investigated and eliminated:
   172	
   173	```json
   174	{"type":"iteration","run":3,"status":"complete","focus":"Connection pooling","findingsCount":3,"newInfoRatio":0.4,"ruledOut":[{"approach":"HTTP/3 multiplexing","reason":"No server-side support in target environment","evidence":"docs.example.com/protocols#supported"}],"keyQuestions":["What causes latency?"],"answeredQuestions":[],"timestamp":"2026-03-18T10:15:00Z","durationMs":52000}
   175	```
   176	
   177	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   178	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
   179	| approach | string | Yes | The approach or hypothesis that was eliminated |
   180	| reason | string | Yes | Why it was ruled out |
   181	| evidence | string | No | Source reference supporting the elimination |
   182	
   183	Iteration files (`research/iterations/iteration-NNN.md`) MUST include `## Ruled Out` and `## Dead Ends` sections when negative knowledge is captured. These sections feed reducer-owned strategy and registry refreshes and prevent future iterations from re-exploring eliminated paths.
   184	
   185	### Novelty Justification
   186	
   187	The optional `noveltyJustification` field provides a human-readable breakdown of what the `newInfoRatio` represents:
   188	
   189	```json
   190	{"type":"iteration","run":3,"status":"complete","focus":"Reconnection strategies","findingsCount":4,"newInfoRatio":0.7,"noveltyJustification":"2 new findings on reconnection backoff, 1 refinement of prior keepalive finding","keyQuestions":["How to handle reconnection?"],"answeredQuestions":[],"timestamp":"2026-03-18T10:15:00Z","durationMs":48000}
   191	```
   192	
   193	This field aids post-hoc analysis and helps subsequent iterations calibrate expectations. It is not used in convergence computation.
   194	
   195	### Focus Track Labels
   196	
   197	The optional `focusTrack` field assigns a grouping label to an iteration for post-hoc analysis:
   198	
   199	```json
   200	{"type":"iteration","run":4,...,"focusTrack":"browser-support",...}
   201	```
   202	
   203	Track labels are free-form strings used for filtering and grouping in dashboards and analysis. They are not used for orchestration or convergence decisions. Multiple iterations may share the same track label.
   204	
   205	### Source Strength
   206	
   207	Iteration files may annotate individual findings with a `sourceStrength` classification:
   208	
   209	| Strength | Meaning | Convergence Impact |
   210	|----------|---------|-------------------|
   211	| `primary` | Authoritative, verified by multiple sources | Counts toward question coverage |
   212	| `secondary` | Corroborating evidence, supports a primary finding | Counts toward question coverage |
   213	| `tentative` | Single source, unverified | Does NOT count toward question coverage |
   214	
   215	In iteration file findings sections, annotate inline: `[Finding text] (sourceStrength: primary)`. In JSONL records, source strength is tracked implicitly through the `newInfoRatio` -- tentative findings contribute less to the ratio. The convergence algorithm excludes tentative-only findings when computing `entropyCoverage`.
   216	
   217	### Extended Status Values
   218	
   219	The iteration `status` field supports the following values:
   220

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md | sed -n '190,220p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   190	#### Step 4: Evaluate Results
   191	After agent completes:
   192	1. Verify `{spec_folder}/research/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md` was created
   193	2. Verify JSONL was appended with iteration record
   194	3. Run reducer with `{ latestJSONLDelta, newIterationFile, priorReducedState }`
   195	4. Verify reducer refreshed `deep-research-strategy.md`, `findings-registry.json`, and `deep-research-dashboard.md`
   196	5. Verify the refreshed packet surfaces reflect the new iteration evidence
   197	6. Extract `newInfoRatio` from JSONL record
   198	7. Track stuck count: skip if `status == "thought"` (no change), reset to 0 if `status == "insight"` (breakthrough counts as progress), increment if `newInfoRatio < config.convergenceThreshold`, reset otherwise
   199	
   200	#### Step 4a: Generate Dashboard
   201	
   202	After evaluating iteration results, generate a human-readable dashboard:
   203	
   204	1. Read JSONL state log, findings registry, and strategy.md
   205	2. Generate or regenerate `research/deep-research-dashboard.md` with the following sections:
   206	   - **Iteration table**: `| run | focus | newInfoRatio | findings count | status |`
   207	   - **Question status**: `X/Y answered` with itemized list (answered vs remaining)
   208	   - **Trend**: Last 3 newInfoRatio values with direction indicator (ascending, descending, flat)
   209	   - **Dead ends**: Consolidated from all iteration `ruledOut` data
   210	   - **Next focus**: Current value from strategy.md
   211	   - **Active risks**: Guard violations, stuck count, budget warnings
   212	3. Log event: `{"type":"event","event":"dashboard_generated","iteration":N}`
   213	4. The dashboard is **auto-generated only** — never manually edited
   214	5. The dashboard file is overwritten each iteration (not appended)
   215	6. Dashboard generation is non-blocking: if it fails, log a warning and continue the loop
   216	
   217	In **confirm mode**, the dashboard is displayed to the user at each iteration approval gate. In **auto mode**, it is written silently for post-hoc review.
   218	
   219	#### Step 4b: Checkpoint Commit (REFERENCE-ONLY)
   220

    13	import { getDefaultConfig } from './indexer-types.js';
    14	import type { IndexerConfig } from './indexer-types.js';
    15	import * as graphDb from './code-graph-db.js';
    16	
    17	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    18	// Types
    19	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20	
    21	export type ReadyAction = 'none' | 'full_scan' | 'selective_reindex';
    22	export type GraphFreshness = 'fresh' | 'stale' | 'empty';
    23	
    24	export interface ReadyResult {
    25	  freshness: GraphFreshness;
    26	  action: ReadyAction;
    27	  files?: string[];
    28	  inlineIndexPerformed: boolean;
    29	  reason: string;
    30	}
    31	
    32	export interface EnsureReadyOptions {
    33	  allowInlineIndex?: boolean;
    34	  allowInlineFullScan?: boolean;
    35	}
    36	
    37	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    38	// Constants
    39	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	
    41	/** Maximum time (ms) before auto-indexing is aborted */
    42	const AUTO_INDEX_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000;
    43	
    44	/** Maximum stale files before we switch from selective to full reindex */
    45	const SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD = 50;
    46	
    47	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    48	// Internal helpers
    49	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50	
    51	function getCurrentGitHead(rootDir: string): string | null {
    52	  try {
    53	    return execSync('git rev-parse HEAD', {
    54	      cwd: rootDir,
    55	      encoding: 'utf-8',
    56	      timeout: 5_000,
    57	      stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
    58	    }).trim();
    59	  } catch {
    60	    return null;
    61	  }
    62	}
    63	
    64	function partitionTrackedFiles(filePaths: string[]): { existingFiles: string[]; deletedFiles: string[] } {
    65	  const existingFiles: string[] = [];
    66	  const deletedFiles: string[] = [];
    67	
    68	  for (const filePath of filePaths) {
    69	    if (existsSync(filePath)) {
    70	      existingFiles.push(filePath);
    71	      continue;
    72	    }
    73	
    74	    deletedFiles.push(filePath);
    75	  }
    76	
    77	  return { existingFiles, deletedFiles };
    78	}
    79	
    80	function cleanupDeletedTrackedFiles(filePaths: string[]): number {
    81	  for (const filePath of filePaths) {
    82	    graphDb.removeFile(filePath);
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  return filePaths.length;
    86	}
    87	
    88	function appendCleanupReason(reason: string, removedDeletedCount: number): string {
    89	  if (removedDeletedCount === 0) {
    90	    return reason;
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  return `${reason}; removed ${removedDeletedCount} deleted tracked file(s)`;
    94	}
    95	
    96	/** Detect graph state without triggering any reindex */
    97	function detectState(rootDir: string): {
    98	  freshness: GraphFreshness;
    99	  action: ReadyAction;
   100	  staleFiles: string[];
   101	  deletedFiles: string[];
   102	  reason: string;
   103	} {
   104	  const d = getDb();
   105	
   106	  // Condition (a): Graph is empty
   107	  const nodeCount = (d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM code_nodes').get() as { c: number }).c;
   108	  if (nodeCount === 0) {
   109	    return { freshness: 'empty', action: 'full_scan', staleFiles: [], deletedFiles: [], reason: 'graph is empty (0 nodes)' };
   110	  }
   111	
   112	  // Condition (b): Git HEAD changed
   113	  const currentHead = getCurrentGitHead(rootDir);
   114	  const storedHead = getLastGitHead();
   115	  const headChanged = Boolean(currentHead && storedHead && currentHead !== storedHead);
   116	  const headChangedReason = headChanged
   117	    ? `git HEAD changed: ${storedHead!.slice(0, 8)} -> ${currentHead!.slice(0, 8)}`
   118	    : null;
   119	
   120	  // Condition (c): Check file mtime drift on tracked files
   121	  const trackedFiles = graphDb.getTrackedFiles();
   122	  if (trackedFiles.length === 0) {
   123	    return { freshness: 'empty', action: 'full_scan', staleFiles: [], deletedFiles: [], reason: 'no tracked files in code_files table' };
   124	  }
   125	
   126	  const { existingFiles, deletedFiles } = partitionTrackedFiles(trackedFiles);
   127	  const { stale } = ensureFreshFiles(existingFiles);
   128	  if (stale.length === 0) {
   129	    if (headChanged) {
   130	      return {
   131	        freshness: 'stale',
   132	        action: 'full_scan',
   133	        staleFiles: [],
   134	        deletedFiles,
   135	        reason: deletedFiles.length > 0
   136	          ? `${headChangedReason}; tracked files appear up-to-date on disk; ${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk`
   137	          : `${headChangedReason}; tracked files appear up-to-date on disk`,
   138	      };
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    if (deletedFiles.length > 0) {
   142	      return {
   143	        freshness: 'stale',
   144	        action: 'none',
   145	        staleFiles: [],
   146	        deletedFiles,
   147	        reason: `${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk`,
   148	      };
   149	    }
   150	
   151	    return { freshness: 'fresh', action: 'none', staleFiles: [], deletedFiles: [], reason: 'all tracked files are up-to-date' };
   152	  }
   153	
   154	  // Too many stale files => full scan is more efficient
   155	  if (stale.length > SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD) {
   156	    return {
   157	      freshness: 'stale',
   158	      action: 'full_scan',
   159	      staleFiles: stale,
   160	      deletedFiles,
   161	      reason: [
   162	        headChangedReason,
   163	        `${stale.length} stale files exceed selective threshold (${SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD})`,
   164	        deletedFiles.length > 0 ? `${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk` : null,
   165	      ].filter(Boolean).join('; '),
   166	    };
   167	  }
   168	
   169	  return {
   170	    freshness: 'stale',
   171	    action: 'selective_reindex',
   172	    staleFiles: stale,
   173	    deletedFiles,
   174	    reason: [
   175	      headChangedReason,
   176	      `${stale.length} file(s) have newer mtime than indexed_at`,
   177	      deletedFiles.length > 0 ? `${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk` : null,
   178	    ].filter(Boolean).join('; '),
   179	  };
   180	}
   181	
   182	/** Run indexFiles with a timeout guard */
   183	async function indexWithTimeout(config: IndexerConfig, timeoutMs: number): Promise<void> {
   184	  const controller = new AbortController();
   185	  const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
   186	
   187	  try {
   188	    const results = await Promise.race([
   189	      indexFiles(config),
   190	      new Promise<never>((_, reject) => {
   191	        controller.signal.addEventListener('abort', () =>
   192	          reject(new Error(`Auto-indexing timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`)),
   193	        );
   194	      }),
   195	    ]);
   196	
   197	    // Persist results to DB
   198	    let persistedDetectorProvenance: ReturnType<typeof graphDb.getLastDetectorProvenance> = null;
   199	    for (const result of results) {
   200	      try {
   201	        const fileId = graphDb.upsertFile(
   202	          result.filePath, result.language, result.contentHash,
   203	          result.nodes.length, result.edges.length,
   204	          result.parseHealth, result.parseDurationMs,
   205	        );
   206	        graphDb.replaceNodes(fileId, result.nodes);
   207	        const sourceIds = result.nodes.map(n => n.symbolId);
   208	        graphDb.replaceEdges(sourceIds, result.edges);
   209	        persistedDetectorProvenance ??= result.detectorProvenance;
   210	      } catch {
   211	        // Best-effort: skip files that fail to persist
   212	      }
   213	    }
   214	
   215	    if (persistedDetectorProvenance) {
   216	      graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenance(persistedDetectorProvenance);
   217	    }
   218	  } finally {
   219	    clearTimeout(timer);
   220	  }
   221	}
   222	
   223	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   224	// Public API
   225	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   226	
   227	/**
   228	 * Check whether the code graph is ready and, if not, perform
   229	 * the minimum necessary reindexing before returning.
   230	 *
   231	 * Has a 10-second timeout guard so auto-indexing never blocks
   232	 * queries forever.
   233	 */
   234	/** Debounce: skip re-check if last check was within this window */
   235	const DEBOUNCE_MS = 5_000;
   236	let lastCheckAt = 0;
   237	let lastCheckResult: ReadyResult | null = null;
   238	
   239	export async function ensureCodeGraphReady(rootDir: string, options: EnsureReadyOptions = {}): Promise<ReadyResult> {
   240	  // Debounce: skip if checked recently (prevents redundant work on rapid queries)
   241	  const now = Date.now();
   242	  if (lastCheckResult && (now - lastCheckAt) < DEBOUNCE_MS) {
   243	    return lastCheckResult;
   244	  }
   245	  lastCheckAt = now;
   246	  const allowInlineIndex = options.allowInlineIndex ?? true;
   247	  const allowInlineFullScan = options.allowInlineFullScan ?? allowInlineIndex;
   248	
   249	  const state = detectState(rootDir);
   250	  const removedDeletedCount = cleanupDeletedTrackedFiles(state.deletedFiles);
   251	
   252	  if (state.action === 'none') {
   253	    lastCheckResult = {
   254	      freshness: state.freshness,
   255	      action: 'none',
   256	      inlineIndexPerformed: false,
   257	      reason: appendCleanupReason(state.reason, removedDeletedCount),
   258	    };
   259	    return lastCheckResult;
   260	  }

 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	  }
   261	
   262	  if (state.action === 'selective_reindex' && !allowInlineIndex) {
   263	    lastCheckResult = {
   264	      freshness: state.freshness,
   265	      action: state.action,
   266	      ...(state.action === 'selective_reindex' ? { files: state.staleFiles } : {}),
   267	      inlineIndexPerformed: false,
   268	      reason: appendCleanupReason(`${state.reason}; inline auto-index skipped for read path`, removedDeletedCount),
   269	    };
   270	    return lastCheckResult;
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  if (state.action === 'full_scan' && !allowInlineFullScan) {
   274	    lastCheckResult = {
   275	      freshness: state.freshness,
   276	      action: state.action,
   277	      inlineIndexPerformed: false,
   278	      reason: appendCleanupReason(`${state.reason}; inline full scan skipped for read path`, removedDeletedCount),
   279	    };
   280	    return lastCheckResult;
   281	  }
   282	
   283	  try {
   284	    if (state.action === 'full_scan') {
   285	      const config = getDefaultConfig(rootDir);
   286	      await indexWithTimeout(config, AUTO_INDEX_TIMEOUT_MS);
   287	
   288	      // Update stored git HEAD after full scan
   289	      const head = getCurrentGitHead(rootDir);
   290	      if (head) setLastGitHead(head);
   291	
   292	      lastCheckResult = {
   293	        freshness: state.freshness,
   294	        action: 'full_scan',
   295	        inlineIndexPerformed: true,
   296	        reason: appendCleanupReason(state.reason, removedDeletedCount),
   297	      };
   298	      return lastCheckResult;
   299	    }
   300	
   301	    // selective_reindex: only re-parse stale files
   302	    if (state.action === 'selective_reindex' && state.staleFiles.length > 0) {
   303	      const config = getDefaultConfig(rootDir);
   304	      // F048: Convert absolute stale file paths to rootDir-relative globs
   305	      config.includeGlobs = state.staleFiles.map(f => relative(rootDir, f));
   306	      await indexWithTimeout(config, AUTO_INDEX_TIMEOUT_MS);
   307	
   308	      const head = getCurrentGitHead(rootDir);
   309	      if (head) setLastGitHead(head);
   310	
   311	      lastCheckResult = {
   312	        freshness: state.freshness,
   313	        action: 'selective_reindex',
   314	        files: state.staleFiles,
   315	        inlineIndexPerformed: true,
   316	        reason: appendCleanupReason(state.reason, removedDeletedCount),
   317	      };
   318	      return lastCheckResult;
   319	    }
   320	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   321	    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   322	    console.error(`[ensure-ready] Auto-index failed: ${msg}`);
   323	    lastCheckResult = {
   324	      freshness: state.freshness,
   325	      action: state.action,
   326	      files: state.staleFiles,
   327	      inlineIndexPerformed: false,
   328	      reason: appendCleanupReason(`${state.reason} (auto-index failed: ${msg})`, removedDeletedCount),
   329	    };
   330	    return lastCheckResult;
   331	  }
   332	
   333	  lastCheckResult = {
   334	    freshness: state.freshness,
   335	    action: 'none',
   336	    inlineIndexPerformed: false,
   337	    reason: appendCleanupReason(state.reason, removedDeletedCount),
   338	  };
   339	  return lastCheckResult;
   340	}
   341	
   342	/**
   343	 * Non-mutating freshness check for status display.
   344	 * Does NOT trigger reindexing.
   345	 */
   346	export function getGraphFreshness(rootDir: string): GraphFreshness {
   347	  try {
   348	    const state = detectState(rootDir);
   349	    return state.freshness;
   350	  } catch {
   351	    return 'empty';
   352	  }
   353	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
   643	 * Format a dashboard report as human-readable text.
   644	 *
   645	 * @param report - DashboardReport to format.
   646	 * @returns Multi-line plain text report.
   647	 */
   648	export function formatReportText(report: DashboardReport): string {
   649	  const lines: string[] = [];
   650	
   651	  lines.push('='.repeat(60));
   652	  lines.push('  EVAL REPORTING DASHBOARD');
   653	  lines.push('='.repeat(60));
   654	  lines.push(`Generated: ${report.generatedAt}`);
   655	  lines.push(`Total eval runs: ${report.totalEvalRuns}`);
   656	  lines.push(`Total metric snapshots: ${report.totalSnapshots}`);
   657	  lines.push('');
   658	
   659	  // Summary
   660	  lines.push('SUMMARY');
   661	  lines.push('-'.repeat(40));
   662	  lines.push(report.summary);
   663	  lines.push('');
   664	
   665	  // Per-sprint details
   666	  for (const sprint of report.sprints) {
   667	    lines.push(`SPRINT: ${sprint.sprint}`);
   668	    lines.push('-'.repeat(40));
   669	    lines.push(`  Eval runs: ${sprint.evalRunCount}`);
   670	    lines.push(`  Period: ${sprint.firstSeen} - ${sprint.lastSeen}`);
   671	
   672	    // Metrics
   673	    const metricNames = Object.keys(sprint.metrics).sort();
   674	    if (metricNames.length > 0) {
   675	      lines.push('  Metrics:');
   676	      for (const name of metricNames) {
   677	        const m = sprint.metrics[name];
   678	        lines.push(
   679	          `    ${name.padEnd(35)} latest=${m.latest.toFixed(4)}  mean=${m.mean.toFixed(4)}  range=[${m.min.toFixed(4)}, ${m.max.toFixed(4)}]  n=${m.count}`
   680	        );
   681	      }
   682	    }
   683	
   684	    const channelMetricNames = Object.keys(sprint.channelMetrics).sort();
   685	    if (channelMetricNames.length > 0) {
   686	      lines.push('  Metric Channels:');
   687	      for (const metricName of channelMetricNames) {
   688	        const channelsForMetric = sprint.channelMetrics[metricName];
   689	        const metricChannelNames = Object.keys(channelsForMetric).sort();
   690	        for (const channelName of metricChannelNames) {
   691	          const summary = channelsForMetric[channelName];
   692	          lines.push(
   693	            `    ${`${metricName} [${channelName}]`.padEnd(35)} latest=${summary.latest.toFixed(4)}  mean=${summary.mean.toFixed(4)}  range=[${summary.min.toFixed(4)}, ${summary.max.toFixed(4)}]  n=${summary.count}`
   694	          );
   695	        }
   696	      }
   697	    }
   698	
   699	    // Channels
   700	    const channelNames = Object.keys(sprint.channels).sort();
   701	    if (channelNames.length > 0) {
   702	      lines.push('  Channels:');
   703	      for (const ch of channelNames) {
   704	        const c = sprint.channels[ch];
   705	        lines.push(
   706	          `    ${ch.padEnd(15)} hits=${c.hitCount}  avgLatency=${c.avgLatencyMs.toFixed(1)}ms  queries=${c.queryCount}`
   707	        );
   708	      }
   709	    }
   710	
   711	    lines.push('');
   712	  }
   713	
   714	  // Trends
   715	  if (report.trends.length > 0) {
   716	    lines.push('TRENDS');
   717	    lines.push('-'.repeat(40));
   718	    for (const t of report.trends) {
   719	      const arrow = t.direction === 'improved' ? '+' : t.direction === 'regressed' ? '-' : '=';
   720	      lines.push(
   721	        `  [${arrow}] ${t.metric.padEnd(30)} ${t.previous.toFixed(4)} -> ${t.current.toFixed(4)}  (${t.percentChange >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${t.percentChange.toFixed(1)}%)  ${t.previousLabel} -> ${t.currentLabel}`
   722	      );
   723	    }
   724	    lines.push('');
   725	  }
   726	
   727	  lines.push('='.repeat(60));
   728	  return lines.join('\n');
   729	}

   731	/**
